original.subject: Re: [Unified-system-Core/Unified_System_Core] Add VASER Super-Admin OpenAPI manifest and supporting docs (PR #45)
original.body:
@Copilot commented on this pull request.

## Pull request overview

This PR adds comprehensive documentation for the VASER Super-Admin system, establishing the OpenAPI contract, architecture, security policies, and operational guidelines for an AI-powered network administration platform. The documentation defines how Custom GPT can interact with network devices, Home Assistant, local gateways, cloud services, and management functions through a unified API.

**Changes:**
- Added OpenAPI 3.0 manifest defining 25 action endpoints across network operations, Home Assistant integration, local gateway, cloud services, management, and analytics
- Added architecture documentation describing VASER-Hub components (Action Router, Policy Engine, Credential Vault, Execution Adapters, Inventory, and Audit Logging)
- Added security policy with confirmation matrix, command allowlists, backup rules, and audit logging requirements
- Added role instructions and product plan documents

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 10 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| docs/roles/vaser_super_admin.md | Defines the AI Network Administrator role with responsibilities, safety rules, and response expectations |
| docs/product/vaser_product_plan.md | Outlines product scope, target users, MVP features, roadmap, and presentation structure |
| docs/policies/vaser_security_policy.md | Establishes security requirements including risk-based confirmation matrix and audit logging |
| docs/openapi/vaser_super_admin.yaml | Provides complete OpenAPI 3.0.3 specification with 25 endpoints, security schemes, and request/response schemas |
| docs/architecture/vaser_hub.md | Documents VASER-Hub architecture with component descriptions and execution flow diagrams |
</details>






> +          type: string
+        message:
+          type: string
+      required: [remind_at, message]
+    ReminderResponse:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        reminder_id:
+          type: string
+        status:
+          type: string
+    ReportRequest:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        report_type:
+          type: string
+        timeframe:
+          type: string
+      required: [report_type]
+    ReportResponse:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        report_id:
+          type: string
+        status:
+          type: string
+        download_url:
+          type: string
+    LogCollectRequest:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        sources:
+          type: array
+          items:
+            type: string
+        since:
+          type: string
+        until:
+          type: string
+      required: [sources]
+    LogCollectResponse:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        collection_id:
+          type: string
+        status:
+          type: string
+    LogAnalyzeRequest:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        collection_id:
+          type: string
+        focus:
+          type: array
+          items:
+            type: string
+      required: [collection_id]
+    LogAnalyzeResponse:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        summary:
+          type: string
+        incidents:
+          type: array
+          items:
+            type: string
+    ProjectSummaryRequest:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        project_id:
+          type: string
+        timeframe:
+          type: string
+      required: [project_id]
+    ProjectSummaryResponse:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        summary:
+          type: string
+        risks:
+          type: array
+          items:
+            type: string
+    PresentationRequest:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        content_json:
+          type: object
+          additionalProperties: true
+        format:
+          type: string
+          enum: [pptx, pdf]
+      required: [content_json]
+    PresentationResponse:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        status:
+          type: string
+        file_url:
+          type: string

Timestamp fields like start_time, end_time, last_seen, due_at, remind_at, since, and until are defined as type string without a format specification. Consider adding format: date-time to these fields to specify they should follow RFC 3339 date-time format (e.g., "2024-01-25T10:30:00Z"). This improves API clarity and enables better validation.

> +| --- | --- | --- | --- |
+| scan_network | Medium | Yes | Reveals device inventory. |
+| get_device_info | Low | No | Read-only. |
+| run_command | High | Yes | Restricted by allowlist. |
+| add_device | Medium | Yes | Adds inventory + credentials. |
+| remove_device | High | Yes | Destructive change. |
+| reboot_device | High | Yes | Disruptive. |
+| configure_device | High | Yes | Configuration change. |
+| ha.service_call | Medium | Conditional | Confirm if service is destructive (e.g., reboot). |
+| ha.get_state | Low | No | Read-only. |
+| ha.set_state | Medium | Conditional | Confirm if impacts safety (locks, alarms). |
+| ha.execute_script | Medium | Conditional | Confirm if script is marked critical. |
+| local.run | High | Yes | Restricted by allowlist. |
+| local.read | Low | No | Read-only. |
+| local.write | Medium | Yes | Data modification. |
+| cloud.* | Medium | Conditional | Confirm for data deletion or external share. |

The confirmation matrix uses wildcard notation "cloud.*" to cover multiple cloud actions. While this is convenient shorthand, it could be ambiguous since it's unclear whether this pattern matches only the three defined cloud operations (cloud.create_calendar_event, cloud.send_mail, cloud.upload_file) or if it's intended to cover future cloud actions. Consider explicitly listing all three cloud actions for clarity, or add a note explaining that the wildcard covers current and future cloud operations.
```suggestion
| cloud.create_calendar_event | Medium | Conditional | Confirm if event includes sensitive details or external attendees. |
| cloud.send_mail | Medium | Conditional | Confirm for data deletion, sensitive content, or external recipients. |
| cloud.upload_file | Medium | Conditional | Confirm for data deletion or external share. |
```

> +  /management/report/generate:
+    post:
+      tags: [Management]
+      summary: Generate a report.
+      operationId: generate_report
+      security:
+        - ApiKeyAuth: []
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: '#/components/schemas/ReportRequest'
+      responses:
+        '200':
+          description: Report generated.
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: '#/components/schemas/ReportResponse'
+  /analytics/logs/collect:
+    post:
+      tags: [Analytics]
+      summary: Collect system logs.
+      operationId: collect_logs
+      security:
+        - ApiKeyAuth: []
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: '#/components/schemas/LogCollectRequest'
+      responses:
+        '200':
+          description: Logs collected.
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LogCollectResponse'
+  /analytics/logs/analyze:
+    post:
+      tags: [Analytics]
+      summary: Analyze collected logs.
+      operationId: analyze_logs
+      security:
+        - ApiKeyAuth: []
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: '#/components/schemas/LogAnalyzeRequest'
+      responses:
+        '200':
+          description: Analysis results.
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LogAnalyzeResponse'
+  /analytics/project/summarize:
+    post:
+      tags: [Analytics]
+      summary: Summarize project status.
+      operationId: summarize_project
+      security:
+        - ApiKeyAuth: []
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: '#/components/schemas/ProjectSummaryRequest'
+      responses:
+        '200':
+          description: Summary result.
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: '#/components/schemas/ProjectSummaryResponse'
+  /analytics/presentation/generate:
+    post:
+      tags: [Analytics]
+      summary: Generate a presentation.
+      operationId: generate_presentation
+      security:
+        - ApiKeyAuth: []
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: '#/components/schemas/PresentationRequest'
+      responses:
+        '200':
+          description: Presentation generated.
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: '#/components/schemas/PresentationResponse'

All endpoints use POST method, including read-only operations like get_device_info, ha.get_state, local.read, analyze_logs, and summarize_project. While this might be intentional for Custom GPT integration (which may require POST for actions), it deviates from REST best practices where GET should be used for read-only operations. If this is a design constraint for Custom GPT, consider adding a comment in the OpenAPI description explaining why POST is used for all operations.

> +          type: string
+        message:
+          type: string
+      required: [remind_at, message]
+    ReminderResponse:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        reminder_id:
+          type: string
+        status:
+          type: string
+    ReportRequest:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        report_type:
+          type: string
+        timeframe:
+          type: string
+      required: [report_type]
+    ReportResponse:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        report_id:
+          type: string
+        status:
+          type: string
+        download_url:
+          type: string
+    LogCollectRequest:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        sources:
+          type: array
+          items:
+            type: string
+        since:
+          type: string
+        until:
+          type: string
+      required: [sources]
+    LogCollectResponse:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        collection_id:
+          type: string
+        status:
+          type: string
+    LogAnalyzeRequest:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        collection_id:
+          type: string
+        focus:
+          type: array
+          items:
+            type: string
+      required: [collection_id]
+    LogAnalyzeResponse:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        summary:
+          type: string
+        incidents:
+          type: array
+          items:
+            type: string
+    ProjectSummaryRequest:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        project_id:
+          type: string
+        timeframe:
+          type: string
+      required: [project_id]
+    ProjectSummaryResponse:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        summary:
+          type: string
+        risks:
+          type: array
+          items:
+            type: string
+    PresentationRequest:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        content_json:
+          type: object
+          additionalProperties: true
+        format:
+          type: string
+          enum: [pptx, pdf]
+      required: [content_json]
+    PresentationResponse:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        status:
+          type: string
+        file_url:
+          type: string

The architecture document states that "All actions must be tagged with a request_id for traceability" but the OpenAPI specification does not include a request_id field in any request or response schemas. This creates an inconsistency between the documented architecture requirement and the API contract. Consider adding request_id to all request schemas or as a common header parameter to align the API with the traceability requirement.

> +| create_task | Low | No | Read-only impact. |
+| complete_task | Low | No | Read-only impact. |
+| remind_user | Low | No | Read-only impact. |
+| generate_report | Low | No | Read-only impact. |

The confirmation matrix lists create_task, complete_task, remind_user, and generate_report as having "Read-only impact" in the Notes column. However, these actions create or modify data (tasks, reminders, reports), so they are not truly read-only operations. The note appears to be inaccurate. Consider changing the notes to something like "Low impact mutation" or "Non-destructive write operation" to more accurately describe these actions.
```suggestion
| create_task | Low | No | Low impact mutation. |
| complete_task | Low | No | Low impact mutation. |
| remind_user | Low | No | Low impact mutation. |
| generate_report | Low | No | Low impact mutation. |
```

> +  /management/report/generate:
+    post:
+      tags: [Management]
+      summary: Generate a report.
+      operationId: generate_report
+      security:
+        - ApiKeyAuth: []
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: '#/components/schemas/ReportRequest'
+      responses:
+        '200':
+          description: Report generated.
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: '#/components/schemas/ReportResponse'
+  /analytics/logs/collect:
+    post:
+      tags: [Analytics]
+      summary: Collect system logs.
+      operationId: collect_logs
+      security:
+        - ApiKeyAuth: []
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: '#/components/schemas/LogCollectRequest'
+      responses:
+        '200':
+          description: Logs collected.
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LogCollectResponse'
+  /analytics/logs/analyze:
+    post:
+      tags: [Analytics]
+      summary: Analyze collected logs.
+      operationId: analyze_logs
+      security:
+        - ApiKeyAuth: []
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: '#/components/schemas/LogAnalyzeRequest'
+      responses:
+        '200':
+          description: Analysis results.
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LogAnalyzeResponse'
+  /analytics/project/summarize:
+    post:
+      tags: [Analytics]
+      summary: Summarize project status.
+      operationId: summarize_project
+      security:
+        - ApiKeyAuth: []
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: '#/components/schemas/ProjectSummaryRequest'
+      responses:
+        '200':
+          description: Summary result.
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: '#/components/schemas/ProjectSummaryResponse'
+  /analytics/presentation/generate:
+    post:
+      tags: [Analytics]
+      summary: Generate a presentation.
+      operationId: generate_presentation
+      security:
+        - ApiKeyAuth: []
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: '#/components/schemas/PresentationRequest'
+      responses:
+        '200':
+          description: Presentation generated.
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: '#/components/schemas/PresentationResponse'

The OpenAPI specification only defines success responses (200) for all endpoints but does not include error responses (4xx, 5xx). This is incomplete API documentation. Consider adding standard error response definitions such as 400 (Bad Request), 401 (Unauthorized), 403 (Forbidden), 404 (Not Found), and 500 (Internal Server Error) to provide complete API contract information for consumers.

> +    LocalReadRequest:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        path:
+          type: string
+        encoding:
+          type: string
+      required: [path]
+    LocalReadResponse:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        content:
+          type: string
+    LocalWriteRequest:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        path:
+          type: string
+        content:
+          type: string
+        encoding:
+          type: string
+      required: [path, content]

The LocalReadRequest and LocalWriteRequest schemas accept arbitrary path values without any documented constraints or patterns. This could potentially allow path traversal attacks (e.g., "../../etc/passwd"). While the Policy Engine may handle this validation, the API specification should document expected path patterns or constraints to guide implementers and consumers. Consider adding a pattern constraint, description of allowed paths, or reference to the security policy for path validation rules.

> +      type: object
+      properties:
+        message_id:
+          type: string
+        status:
+          type: string
+    CloudUploadRequest:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        provider:
+          type: string
+          enum: [google_drive, icloud_drive, yandex_disk, dropbox]
+        path:
+          type: string
+        content_base64:
+          type: string

The CloudUploadRequest schema accepts content_base64 as a string without any size constraints (maxLength). This could allow extremely large file uploads that might cause memory issues or denial of service. Consider adding a maxLength constraint or documenting the maximum file size limit in the field description to guide API consumers and protect against resource exhaustion.
```suggestion
          type: string
          format: byte
          description: >
            Base64-encoded file content. Maximum supported file size is approximately
            10 MB (corresponding to a Base64 string length of up to 14,000,000
            characters). Larger uploads may be rejected to protect the service from
            resource exhaustion.
          maxLength: 14000000
```

> @@ -0,0 +1,31 @@
+# VASER Super-ИИ-Admin Role Instructions

The title mixes Cyrillic characters "ИИ" (Russian for "AI") with English text. This creates an inconsistent naming convention where the product name appears as "VASER Super-ИИ-Admin" instead of being fully in English. Consider using either "VASER Super-AI-Admin" or "VASER Super Admin" for consistency with English documentation.
```suggestion
# VASER Super-AI-Admin Role Instructions
```

> +  title: VASER Super-ИИ-Admin Actions
+  version: 0.1.0
+  description: >-
+    Unified OpenAPI manifest for VASER Super-ИИ-Admin actions used by Custom GPT.

The title and description use Cyrillic characters "ИИ" (Russian for "AI") mixed with English text. This creates inconsistent naming where the API appears as "VASER Super-ИИ-Admin Actions" instead of being fully in English. Consider using "VASER Super-AI-Admin Actions" or "VASER Super Admin Actions" for consistency with standard OpenAPI documentation practices.
```suggestion
  title: VASER Super-AI-Admin Actions
  version: 0.1.0
  description: >-
    Unified OpenAPI manifest for VASER Super-AI-Admin actions used by Custom GPT.
```

-- 
Reply to this email directly or view it on GitHub:
https://github.com/Unified-system-Core/Unified_System_Core/pull/45#pullrequestreview-3704150683
You are receiving this because you modified the open/close state.

original.subject: Re: [Unified-system-Core/Unified_System_Core] Add VASER Super Admin role policy documentation (PR #44)
original.body:
@Copilot commented on this pull request.

## Pull request overview

This PR introduces comprehensive role documentation for the VASER Super Admin, establishing formal policies for the Chief AI network administrator role within the VASER platform. The documentation defines authority boundaries, human-in-the-loop confirmation requirements, and operational safety protocols.

**Changes:**
- Added `VASER_SUPER_ADMIN.md` role policy document defining scope of authority, confirmation policies, protected nodes, and operational requirements
- Established clear delineation between automated operations and those requiring human approval
- Documented backup, audit logging, and rollback requirements for sensitive operations





> @@ -0,0 +1,92 @@
+# VASER Super Admin Role Policy
+
+## Role Statement
+**Role:** Chief AI network administrator of the VASER platform network.
+
+**Mission:** Ensure stable, secure, and auditable operation of the entire VASER ecosystem, including VASER-Router (Vaser Control Hub), device fleet, automation services, cloud integrations, and user-facing workflows.

Inconsistent capitalization of "VASER-Router" vs "Vaser Control Hub". The document uses "VASER-Router" in line 38 and 91, but here it's written as "VASER-Router (Vaser Control Hub)" with different capitalization for "Vaser". Consider standardizing to either "VASER-Router (VASER Control Hub)" or using consistent casing throughout the document.

> +- Credential or access changes (e.g., SSH keys, API tokens, ACLs, firewall rules).
+- Cloud actions that create, delete, or modify data (Drive, Dropbox, iCloud, Gmail/Outlook, Calendar).
+- Home Assistant actions that change state or trigger automations: **ha.service_call**, **ha.set_state**, **ha.execute_script**.
+- Local actions that write or execute code: **/local/run**, **/local/write**.
+- Reporting actions that expose sensitive data (logs with secrets, user data, or credential traces).
+
+### No Confirmation Needed (Safe Automation)
+- Read-only inventory and diagnostics: **get_device_info**, status checks, health monitoring.
+- Read-only Home Assistant state queries: **ha.get_state**.
+- Read-only local operations: **/local/read**.
+- Non-destructive analytics: **collect_logs**, **analyze_logs**, **summarize_project**.
+- Task/report generation that does not alter systems: **create_task**, **generate_report**, **generate_presentation** (when data sources are read-only and approved).
+
+## Protected Nodes & Critical Commands
+### Protected Nodes (High Sensitivity)
+- **VASER-Router (Vaser Control Hub)**: central orchestration and credential vault.

Inconsistent capitalization of "Vaser Control Hub". Line 6 uses "Vaser Control Hub" while this line uses the same parenthetical. For consistency, consider using "VASER Control Hub" to match the "VASER-Router" naming convention throughout the document.

> +- Rollback artifacts (config snapshots, firmware rollbacks, access restoration scripts) must be verified.
+- Rollbacks also require confirmation unless triggered by an automated safety policy (e.g., system outage detection).
+
+## Automation Boundaries
+### Allowed Without Confirmation
+- Continuous monitoring and passive telemetry collection.
+- Read-only diagnostics and status reports.
+- Log collection and analysis where data is sanitized.
+
+### Not Allowed Without Confirmation
+- Any action that mutates state, modifies access, or impacts availability.
+- Execution of arbitrary commands on production nodes.
+- Changes to credentials, network topology, or control-plane services.
+
+## Notes
+- All actions are executed through **VASER-Router (Vaser Control Hub)** to enforce policy, identity, and audit compliance.

Inconsistent capitalization of "Vaser Control Hub". This line uses "Vaser Control Hub" while "VASER-Router" uses all caps for "VASER". For consistency, consider using "VASER Control Hub" throughout the document.
```suggestion
- All actions are executed through **VASER-Router (VASER Control Hub)** to enforce policy, identity, and audit compliance.
```

--

original.subject: Re: [Unified-system-Core/Unified_System_Core] Add VASER Super Admin OpenAPI spec and supporting documentation (PR #38)
original.body:
@Copilot commented on this pull request.

## Pull request overview

Adds a unified OpenAPI manifest and supporting documentation to define the “VASER Super Admin” role, its safety/approval principles, and the VASER Control Hub architecture/product plan for GPT-driven operations.

**Changes:**
- Introduces a consolidated OpenAPI spec covering network, Home Assistant, local, cloud, management, analytics, and presentation actions.
- Adds role/policy documentation for “VASER Super Admin” including confirmation and credential-handling principles.
- Adds architecture and product/presentation planning docs for VASER Control Hub.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| Agent_Context/Knowledge_Base/Docs/VASER_SUPER_ADMIN_OPENAPI.yaml | New unified OpenAPI spec defining endpoints, schemas, and security schemes for VASER actions. |
| Agent_Context/Knowledge_Base/Docs/VASER_SUPER_ADMIN_ROLE.md | Defines role responsibilities, safety/approval rules, and credential handling expectations. |
| Agent_Context/Knowledge_Base/Docs/VASER_HUB_ARCHITECTURE.md | Documents Control Hub layers, data flow, and security model. |
| Agent_Context/Knowledge_Base/Docs/VASER_PRODUCT_PLAN.md | Outlines MVP/productization roadmap and presentation structure. |





> +- All secrets are stored in VASER Control Hub, referenced by `credentials_ref`.
+- Never expose secrets in plain text; only use references in requests.

This section states that requests should “only use references” via `credentials_ref`, but in the OpenAPI spec `credentials_ref` only appears on device registration and not on most action requests (mail/calendar/local/etc.). To avoid confusing implementers, clarify when `credentials_ref` is expected (e.g., only during initial connector/account binding, with subsequent calls referencing stored `device_id`/account IDs).
```suggestion
- All secrets are stored in VASER Control Hub. During initial device or connector/account registration, they are referenced via `credentials_ref`.
- Never expose secrets in plain text. After registration, action requests (mail, calendar, local, etc.) must not include raw credentials and should instead rely on stored identifiers such as `device_id` and account IDs that are already bound to the appropriate credentials.
```

> @@ -0,0 +1,922 @@
+openapi: 3.1.0

If this spec is intended to be imported as “Custom GPT actions”, consider switching from OpenAPI `3.1.0` to `3.0.x` (commonly required by action importers and some OpenAPI tooling). Using 3.1 can cause schema import/validation failures even when the YAML itself is valid.
```suggestion
openapi: 3.0.3
```

> +      tags: [home_assistant]
+      operationId: ha.service_call
+      summary: Call a Home Assistant service.

Several `operationId` values include dots (e.g., `ha.service_call`). Many tool/action runtimes derive function names directly from `operationId` and only accept `[A-Za-z0-9_-]`, so dotted names can break codegen/action import. Consider normalizing these to a consistent, dot-free format (e.g., `ha_service_call`) across the spec.

> +
+    PresentationResponse:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        status:
+          type: string
+          enum: [ok, error]
+        file_url:
+          type: string
+        slides_count:
+          type: integer
+      required: [status]
+
+security:
+  - bearerAuth: []
+    apiKeyAuth: []

The global `security` requirement currently lists both `bearerAuth` and `apiKeyAuth` in the same requirement object, which means requests must satisfy *both* schemes (logical AND). If the intent is “either bearer token OR API key”, represent this as two separate entries in the `security` array (logical OR), or clarify in the docs that both credentials are mandatory.
```suggestion
  - apiKeyAuth: []
```

> +    DeviceInfo:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        device_id:
+          type: string
+        hostname:
+          type: string
+        ip:
+          type: string

`DeviceInfo` doesn’t declare any required fields, but it’s used by `/network/scan` responses and then `DeviceRef` (required `device_id`) is used by follow-up calls. As written, a valid `NetworkScanResponse` could omit `device_id` entirely, making it impossible for clients to legally reference scanned devices. Consider adding `required` fields to `DeviceInfo` (at least `device_id` and/or `ip`) to make the contract internally consistent.

-- 
Reply to this email directly or view it on GitHub:
https://github.com/Unified-system-Core/Unified_System_Core/pull/38#pullrequestreview-3704147876
You are receiving this because you authored the thread.

original.subject: Re: [Unified-system-Core/Unified_System_Core] Add VASER Super-Admin spec and OpenAPI manifest for Custom GPT actions (PR #36)
original.body:
@Copilot commented on this pull request.

## Pull request overview

This pull request adds comprehensive documentation for the VASER Super-Admin Custom GPT, defining an extensive action surface for managing network devices, Home Assistant integrations, local gateway operations, cloud services, and presentation generation. The PR consists of an OpenAPI 3.0.3 specification and accompanying markdown documentation that outlines the architecture, security policies, and productization roadmap.

**Changes:**
- Added OpenAPI 3.0.3 specification defining 30+ API endpoints across 7 categories (network, Home Assistant, local, cloud, management, analytics, presentations)
- Added comprehensive documentation describing the Super-Admin GPT role, VASER Control Hub architecture, security policies, operational guardrails, and productization timeline

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 12 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| Projects/AI_Core/docs/vaser_super_admin_openapi.yaml | Complete OpenAPI specification with typed schemas for all request/response payloads, covering device management, command execution, Home Assistant control, local operations, cloud integrations, and presentation generation |
| Projects/AI_Core/docs/VASER_SUPER_ADMIN.md | Architectural documentation defining the GPT role, responsibilities, security model with confirmation rules, and three-phase productization plan |





> +    delete:
+      tags: [network]
+      operationId: remove_device
+      summary: Remove a device from VASER Hub.
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: "#/components/schemas/DeviceTargetRequest"
+      responses:
+        "200":
+          description: Device removal result.
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: "#/components/schemas/DeviceMutationResponse"

Using HTTP DELETE with a request body is not considered a RESTful best practice and may not be supported by some HTTP clients or intermediaries. Consider using POST for this operation or passing the device identifier as a path parameter or query parameter instead.

> +        ip_address:
+          type: string
+        protocol:
+          type: string
+          enum: [ssh, winrm, api]
+        command:
+          type: string
+        arguments:
+          type: array
+          items:
+            type: string
+        timeout_seconds:
+          type: integer
+          minimum: 1
+          maximum: 600
+      required: [command]

The RunCommandRequest schema only requires the command field but doesn't require device identification (device_id or ip_address). This could lead to ambiguous requests where it's unclear which device should execute the command. Consider making device identification mandatory, either through a oneOf constraint similar to DeviceInfoRequest or by requiring at least one identifier field.
```suggestion
      required:
        - command
      oneOf:
        - required:
            - device_id
        - required:
            - ip_address
```

> +        ip_address:
+          type: string
+      oneOf:
+        - required: [device_id]
+        - required: [ip_address]
+    ConfigureDeviceRequest:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        device_id:
+          type: string
+        ip_address:
+          type: string
+        config:
+          type: object
+          additionalProperties: true
+      required: [config]

The ConfigureDeviceRequest schema only requires the config field but doesn't require device identification (device_id or ip_address). This creates ambiguity about which device should be configured. Consider adding a oneOf constraint to require either device_id or ip_address, similar to the DeviceTargetRequest schema.
```suggestion
      required: [config]
      oneOf:
        - required: [device_id]
        - required: [ip_address]
```

> +- **Home Assistant**: ha.service_call, ha.get_state, ha.set_state,
+  ha.execute_script.

Inconsistent naming convention between documentation and OpenAPI specification. The markdown documentation lists Home Assistant actions using dot notation (ha.service_call, ha.get_state, ha.set_state, ha.execute_script) while the OpenAPI uses underscore notation (ha_service_call, ha_get_state, ha_set_state, ha_execute_script). Consider using consistent naming across both documents, preferably the underscore convention which matches the operationId format.
```suggestion
- **Home Assistant**: ha_service_call, ha_get_state, ha_set_state,
  ha_execute_script.
```

> +    LocalRunRequest:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        command:
+          type: string
+        arguments:
+          type: array
+          items:
+            type: string
+        timeout_seconds:
+          type: integer
+          minimum: 1
+          maximum: 600
+      required: [command]

The LocalRunRequest schema accepts arbitrary commands without validation constraints. This presents a significant security risk as it could allow execution of any system command. Consider adding an enum of allowed commands or implementing a whitelist pattern, or at minimum add a maxLength constraint and pattern validation to limit potential abuse.

> +    RunCommandRequest:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        device_id:
+          type: string
+        ip_address:
+          type: string
+        protocol:
+          type: string
+          enum: [ssh, winrm, api]
+        command:
+          type: string
+        arguments:
+          type: array
+          items:
+            type: string
+        timeout_seconds:
+          type: integer
+          minimum: 1
+          maximum: 600
+      required: [command]

The RunCommandRequest schema accepts arbitrary commands without validation constraints. For remote command execution on managed devices, this presents a significant security risk. Consider adding constraints such as command whitelisting, pattern validation, or at minimum a maxLength to prevent command injection attacks and limit the attack surface.

> +        scope:
+          type: string
+        filters:
+          type: object
+          additionalProperties: true
+      required: [report_type]
+    ReportResponse:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        status:
+          type: string
+        report_id:
+          type: string
+        summary:
+          type: string
+        url:
+          type: string
+      required: [status, report_id]
+
+    CollectLogsRequest:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        sources:
+          type: array
+          items:
+            type: string
+        since:
+          type: string
+          format: date-time
+      required: [sources]
+    CollectLogsResponse:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        status:
+          type: string
+        log_bundle_id:
+          type: string
+        stored_at:
+          type: string
+      required: [status, log_bundle_id]
+
+    AnalyzeLogsRequest:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        log_bundle_id:
+          type: string
+        analysis_type:
+          type: string
+          enum: [incident, anomaly, performance, security, custom]
+      required: [log_bundle_id]
+    AnalyzeLogsResponse:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        status:
+          type: string
+        findings:
+          type: array
+          items:
+            type: string
+        severity:
+          type: string
+      required: [status]
+
+    SummarizeProjectRequest:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        focus:
+          type: string
+        audience:
+          type: string
+      required: [focus]
+    SummarizeProjectResponse:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        status:
+          type: string
+        summary:
+          type: string
+      required: [status, summary]
+
+    GeneratePresentationRequest:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        content_json:
+          type: object
+          additionalProperties: true
+        format:
+          type: string
+          enum: [pptx, pdf]
+      required: [content_json]
+    GeneratePresentationResponse:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        status:
+          type: string
+        presentation_id:
+          type: string
+        url:
+          type: string
+      required: [status, presentation_id]

String fields in request schemas lack basic constraints such as minLength, maxLength, or pattern validation. This could lead to issues with excessively large inputs, empty strings where not expected, or malformed data. Consider adding appropriate constraints to fields like device names, IP addresses (use pattern for IP validation), CIDR blocks (use pattern), entity_ids, script_ids, and other identifier fields.

> +3. Orchestrator executes via protocol adapter.
+4. Response is returned with status and logs.
+
+## Security Policy
+### User Confirmation Required
+- Network scans outside whitelisted ranges.
+- Removing or rebooting devices.
+- Running commands with elevated privileges.
+- Changing device configuration or credentials.
+- Any action that affects billing, security, or external services.
+
+### Auto-Approved Actions
+- Read-only status checks and inventory queries.
+- Non-destructive Home Assistant state reads.
+- Report generation and log summarization.
+

The documentation describes security policies requiring user confirmation for high-risk actions, but the OpenAPI specification does not include any mechanism to indicate which operations require confirmation. Consider adding an x-requires-confirmation extension field to operations that need user approval, or documenting the confirmation requirement in operation descriptions to ensure proper implementation of the policy engine.
```suggestion

### OpenAPI Annotation
All VASER Super-Admin actions are defined in `docs/vaser_super_admin_openapi.yaml`.
High-risk operations that require user confirmation **MUST** set the
`x-requires-confirmation: true` extension on the corresponding OpenAPI
operation object. Auto-approved operations **MUST NOT** set this extension
(or **MUST** set `x-requires-confirmation: false`). The Policy Engine uses
this flag to determine when to prompt the user for confirmation before
executing an action.
```

> +openapi: 3.0.3
+info:
+  title: VASER Super Admin Actions API
+  version: 1.0.0
+  description: |
+    Unified action surface for the VASER Super-Admin Custom GPT. This manifest
+    groups actions across network management, Home Assistant, local gateway,
+    cloud productivity, management workflows, analytics, and presentation
+    generation.
+servers:
+  - url: https://vaser-hub.local/api
+    description: VASER Control Hub (internal)
+  - url: https://vaser-hub.example.com/api
+    description: VASER Control Hub (external)

The OpenAPI specification is missing security scheme definitions. For an API handling sensitive operations like device management, command execution, and Home Assistant control, authentication and authorization mechanisms should be explicitly defined using securitySchemes in the components section and applied to appropriate operations. Consider adding OAuth2, API key, or bearer token authentication schemes.

> +              $ref: "#/components/schemas/ReminderRequest"
+      responses:
+        "200":
+          description: Reminder creation result.
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: "#/components/schemas/GenericActionResponse"
+  /management/reports:
+    post:
+      tags: [management]
+      operationId: generate_report
+      summary: Generate a status or analytics report.
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: "#/components/schemas/ReportRequest"
+      responses:
+        "200":
+          description: Report generation result.
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: "#/components/schemas/ReportResponse"
+
+  /analytics/collect-logs:
+    post:
+      tags: [analytics]
+      operationId: collect_logs
+      summary: Collect logs from VASER Hub and integrations.
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: "#/components/schemas/CollectLogsRequest"
+      responses:
+        "200":
+          description: Log collection result.
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: "#/components/schemas/CollectLogsResponse"
+  /analytics/analyze-logs:
+    post:
+      tags: [analytics]
+      operationId: analyze_logs
+      summary: Analyze collected logs.
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: "#/components/schemas/AnalyzeLogsRequest"
+      responses:
+        "200":
+          description: Analysis result.
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: "#/components/schemas/AnalyzeLogsResponse"
+  /analytics/summarize-project:
+    post:
+      tags: [analytics]
+      operationId: summarize_project
+      summary: Summarize project status.
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: "#/components/schemas/SummarizeProjectRequest"
+      responses:
+        "200":
+          description: Summary result.
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: "#/components/schemas/SummarizeProjectResponse"
+
+  /presentations/generate:
+    post:
+      tags: [presentations]
+      operationId: generate_presentation
+      summary: Generate a presentation (pptx/pdf).
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: "#/components/schemas/GeneratePresentationRequest"
+      responses:
+        "200":
+          description: Presentation generation result.
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: "#/components/schemas/GeneratePresentationResponse"

All API operations only define 200 success responses and lack error response definitions. Production APIs should document common error scenarios such as 400 (Bad Request), 401 (Unauthorized), 403 (Forbidden), 404 (Not Found), and 500 (Internal Server Error) with appropriate response schemas to help API consumers handle errors properly.

> +    LocalReadRequest:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        path:
+          type: string
+      required: [path]
+    LocalReadResponse:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        status:
+          type: string
+        content:
+          type: string
+      required: [status, content]
+    LocalWriteRequest:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        path:
+          type: string
+        content:
+          type: string
+        mode:
+          type: string
+          enum: [overwrite, append]
+      required: [path, content]

The LocalReadRequest and LocalWriteRequest schemas accept arbitrary file paths without any validation constraints (such as pattern matching or path restrictions). This could enable path traversal attacks if not properly validated server-side. Consider adding input validation constraints such as path patterns, allowed directories, or maximum path length to prevent security vulnerabilities.

> +    HAServiceCallRequest:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        domain:
+          type: string
+        service:
+          type: string
+        service_data:
+          type: object
+          additionalProperties: true
+      required: [domain, service]

The HAServiceCallRequest accepts arbitrary domain and service strings without validation constraints. Consider adding pattern validation or enum constraints to limit service calls to known valid Home Assistant domains and services, or at minimum add maxLength constraints to prevent abuse.

-- 
Reply to this email directly or view it on GitHub:
https://github.com/Unified-system-Core/Unified_System_Core/pull/36#pullrequestreview-3704147385
You are receiving this because you authored the thread.

The RunCommandRequest schema only requires the command field but doesn't require device identification (device_id or ip_address). This could lead to ambiguous requests where it's unclear which device should execute the command. Consider making device identification mandatory, either through a oneOf constraint similar to DeviceInfoRequest or by requiring at least one identifier field.
```suggestion
      required:
        - command
      oneOf:
        - required:
            - device_id
        - required:
            - ip_address
```

> +        ip_address:
+          type: string
+      oneOf:
+        - required: [device_id]
+        - required: [ip_address]
+    ConfigureDeviceRequest:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        device_id:
+          type: string
+        ip_address:
+          type: string
+        config:
+          type: object
+          additionalProperties: true
+      required: [config]

The ConfigureDeviceRequest schema only requires the config field but doesn't require device identification (device_id or ip_address). This creates ambiguity about which device should be configured. Consider adding a oneOf constraint to require either device_id or ip_address, similar to the DeviceTargetRequest schema.

original.subject: Re: [Unified-system-Core/Unified_System_Core] Add VASER Super Admin action manifest and docs (PR #37)
original.body:
@Copilot commented on this pull request.

## Pull request overview

This PR introduces comprehensive documentation and an OpenAPI specification for the VASER Super Admin GPT system, which serves as an AI-driven operations platform for network administration, Home Assistant control, and productivity automation.

**Changes:**
- Added a unified OpenAPI 3.1.0 manifest defining 30+ endpoints across network, Home Assistant, local tools, cloud integrations, management, analytics, and presentation generation capabilities
- Introduced Super Admin role documentation outlining mission, responsibilities, operating principles, security policies, and execution workflows
- Added VASER-Hub architecture documentation describing core components (API Gateway, Credential Vault, Execution Orchestrator, Device Inventory, Policy Engine) and data flows
- Created a product plan with MVP scope, integration roadmap, and presentation structure guidelines

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 16 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| Agent_Context/Knowledge_Base/Docs/vaser_super_admin_openapi.yaml | Comprehensive OpenAPI manifest defining all VASER-Hub API endpoints, request/response schemas, and operation specifications |
| Agent_Context/Knowledge_Base/Docs/VASER_SUPER_ADMIN_ROLE.md | Role instruction document defining the Super Admin GPT's mission, responsibilities, security policies, and recommended workflows |
| Agent_Context/Knowledge_Base/Docs/VASER_HUB_ARCHITECTURE.md | Architecture documentation describing VASER-Hub components, data flows, integration adapters, and safety guardrails |
| Agent_Context/Knowledge_Base/Docs/VASER_PRODUCT_PLAN.md | Product plan outlining MVP scope, integration expansion, UX considerations, and presentation structure for stakeholder materials |





> +          default: utf-8
+      additionalProperties: false
+    LocalWriteRequest:
+      type: object
+      required: [path, content]
+      properties:
+        path:
+          type: string
+        content:
+          type: string
+        encoding:
+          type: string
+          default: utf-8
+        overwrite:
+          type: boolean
+          default: false

The overwrite field defaults to false for LocalWriteRequest, which is a safe default. However, there's no indication in the API of what happens when overwrite is false and the file already exists. The response schema (ActionStatus) doesn't include error details. Consider either: (1) documenting the expected error response in the 409 Conflict status code, or (2) enhancing the ActionStatus schema to include error details.
```suggestion
          default: false
          description: >-
            Whether to overwrite the file if it already exists. When set to
            false (the default) and the target path already exists, the server
            MUST NOT overwrite the existing file and MUST instead return an
            error response (typically 409 Conflict with an ActionStatus body
            describing the conflict). When set to true, the existing file
            will be replaced.
```

> +      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: '#/components/schemas/RunCommandRequest'
+      responses:
+        '200':
+          description: Command execution result
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: '#/components/schemas/CommandResult'
+  /ha/service_call:
+    post:
+      operationId: ha.service_call

The operationId "ha.service_call" contains a dot character. While this may be intentional for namespacing, it's unconventional in OpenAPI operationIds and may cause issues with some code generators. Consider using snake_case like "ha_service_call" instead for consistency with other operationIds in this specification.

> +      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: '#/components/schemas/HaServiceCallRequest'
+      responses:

+                $ref: '#/components/schemas/ActionStatus'
+  /ha/get_state:
+    post:
+      operationId: ha.get_state

The operationId "ha.get_state" contains a dot character. While this may be intentional for namespacing, it's unconventional in OpenAPI operationIds and may cause issues with some code generators. Consider using snake_case like "ha_get_state" instead for consistency with other operationIds in this specification.

> +      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: '#/components/schemas/HaStateRequest'
+      responses:
+        '200':
+          description: Current state
+          content:
+            application/json:

+                $ref: '#/components/schemas/HaStateResponse'
+  /ha/set_state:
+    post:
+      operationId: ha.set_state

The operationId "ha.set_state" contains a dot character. While this may be intentional for namespacing, it's unconventional in OpenAPI operationIds and may cause issues with some code generators. Consider using snake_case like "ha_set_state" instead for consistency with other operationIds in this specification.
```suggestion
      operationId: ha_set_state
```

> +      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:

+      responses:

+  /ha/execute_script:

+      operationId: ha.execute_script

The operationId "ha.execute_script" contains a dot character. While this may be intentional for namespacing, it's unconventional in OpenAPI operationIds and may cause issues with some code generators. Consider using snake_case like "ha_execute_script" instead for consistency with other operationIds in this specification.
```suggestion
      operationId: ha_execute_script
```

> +    LocalWriteRequest:
+      type: object
+      required: [path, content]
+      properties:
+        path:
+          type: string
+        content:
+          type: string
+        encoding:
+          type: string
+          default: utf-8
+        overwrite:
+          type: boolean
+          default: false
+      additionalProperties: false

Security concern: The LocalWriteRequest allows writing arbitrary content to any file path with an overwrite flag. This endpoint lacks path validation constraints and could be used to overwrite critical system files. Consider adding: (1) a pattern constraint on the path field to restrict to safe directories, (2) a required "reason" field for audit logging, or (3) file size limits in the schema.

> +        dry_run:
+          type: boolean
+          default: true

The dry_run field defaults to true for ConfigureDeviceRequest, which is a safe default. However, this same safety pattern should be applied consistently to other destructive operations like reboot_device, remove_device, and run_command. Consider adding a dry_run or confirmation field to these endpoints as well for consistency.

> +          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: '#/components/schemas/ReminderRequest'
+      responses:
+        '201':
+          description: Reminder scheduled

+  /management/reports:
+    post:
+      operationId: generate_report
+      summary: Generate a management report
+      tags: [management]
+      requestBody:
+        required: true

+  /analytics/logs/collect:
+    post:
+      operationId: collect_logs
+      summary: Collect system logs
+      tags: [analytics]
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:

+      responses:

+  /analytics/logs/analyze:

+      summary: Analyze collected logs
+      tags: [analytics]
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: '#/components/schemas/LogAnalyzeRequest'
+      responses:
+        '200':
+          description: Analysis output
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LogAnalyzeResponse'
+  /analytics/projects/summarize:
+    post:
+      operationId: summarize_project
+      summary: Summarize project status
+      tags: [analytics]
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: '#/components/schemas/ProjectSummaryRequest'
+      responses:
+        '200':
+          description: Project summary
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: '#/components/schemas/ProjectSummaryResponse'
+  /analytics/presentation:
+    post:
+      operationId: generate_presentation
+      summary: Generate a presentation (pptx/pdf)
+      tags: [presentation]
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: '#/components/schemas/PresentationRequest'
+      responses:
+        '200':
+          description: Presentation generated
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: '#/components/schemas/PresentationResponse'

Missing error response definitions: The OpenAPI specification only defines success responses (200, 201) but does not include common error responses such as 400 (Bad Request), 401 (Unauthorized), 403 (Forbidden), 404 (Not Found), or 500 (Internal Server Error). For a production API, these should be documented to help client developers handle errors appropriately. Consider adding a shared error schema and referencing it in all endpoints.

> +          format: date-time
+        until:
+          type: string
+          format: date-time
+      additionalProperties: false
+    LogAnalyzeRequest:
+      type: object
+      required: [log_bundle_id]
+      properties:
+        log_bundle_id:
+          type: string
+        focus:
+          type: array
+          items:
+            type: string
+      additionalProperties: false
+    LogAnalyzeResponse:
+      type: object
+      properties:

+          type: string
+        summary:
+          type: string
+        findings:
+          type: array
+          items:
+            type: string
+      required: [analysis_id, summary]
+      additionalProperties: false
+    ProjectSummaryRequest:
+      type: object
+      required: [project]
+      properties:
+        project:
+          type: string
+        scope:
+          type: string
+          enum: [architecture, operations, roadmap, risks]
+      additionalProperties: false
+    ProjectSummaryResponse:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        project:
+          type: string
+        summary:
+          type: string
+        next_actions:
+          type: array
+          items:
+            type: string
+      required: [project, summary]
+      additionalProperties: false
+    PresentationRequest:
+      type: object
+      required: [content_json]
+      properties:
+        content_json:
+          type: object
+        output_format:
+          type: string
+          enum: [pptx, pdf]
+          default: pptx
+        theme:
+          type: string
+        locale:
+          type: string
+          default: en
+      additionalProperties: false
+    PresentationResponse:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        presentation_id:
+          type: string
+        status:
+          type: string
+        download_url:
+          type: string
+      required: [presentation_id, status]
+      additionalProperties: false
+    Attachment:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        filename:
+          type: string
+        content_base64:
+          type: string
+          format: byte
+        mime_type:
+          type: string
+      required: [filename, content_base64]
+      additionalProperties: false
+    ActionStatus:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        success:
+          type: boolean
+        message:
+          type: string
+      required: [success]
+      additionalProperties: false

The OpenAPI specification lacks any security scheme definition. Given that this API controls sensitive operations (network device management, Home Assistant control, file system access), authentication and authorization mechanisms should be explicitly defined in the components/securitySchemes section and applied to operations via the security field. Consider adding OAuth2, API Key, or JWT-based authentication schemes.

> +          format: date-time
+        until:
+          type: string
+          format: date-time
+      additionalProperties: false
+    LogAnalyzeRequest:
+      type: object
+      required: [log_bundle_id]

+    ProjectSummaryRequest:
+      type: object
+      required: [project]
+      properties:
+        project:
+          type: string
+        scope:
+          type: string
+          enum: [architecture, operations, roadmap, risks]
+      additionalProperties: false
+    ProjectSummaryResponse:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        project:
+          type: string

+          type: string
+        next_actions:
+          type: array

+            type: string
+      required: [project, summary]
+      additionalProperties: false
+    PresentationRequest:
+      type: object
+      required: [content_json]
+      properties:
+        content_json:
+          type: object
+        output_format:
+          type: string
+          enum: [pptx, pdf]
+          default: pptx
+        theme:
+          type: string
+        locale:
+          type: string
+          default: en
+      additionalProperties: false
+    PresentationResponse:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        presentation_id:
+          type: string
+        status:
+          type: string
+        download_url:
+          type: string
+      required: [presentation_id, status]
+      additionalProperties: false
+    Attachment:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        filename:
+          type: string
+        content_base64:
+          type: string
+          format: byte
+        mime_type:
+          type: string
+      required: [filename, content_base64]
+      additionalProperties: false
+    ActionStatus:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        success:
+          type: boolean
+        message:
+          type: string
+      required: [success]
+      additionalProperties: false

Documentation inconsistency: The VASER_SUPER_ADMIN_ROLE.md mentions that "VASER-Hub owns credentials and executes safely" and the VASER_HUB_ARCHITECTURE.md describes a Credential Vault, but the OpenAPI manifest doesn't indicate how authentication/authorization works. This creates a gap between the conceptual documentation and the API specification. The OpenAPI should reference or align with the security model described in the architecture documentation.

> +servers:
+  - url: https://vaser-hub.local/api
+    description: Primary VASER-Hub gateway
+  - url: http://localhost:8088/api
+    description: Local development gateway

The server URLs reference a ".local" domain (https://vaser-hub.local/api) which uses mDNS/Bonjour and may not be resolvable in all environments. This could cause connectivity issues for clients not on the same local network or in production deployments. Consider adding a note in the description or providing an alternative production server URL example.

> +        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: '#/components/schemas/MailSendRequest'
+      responses:
+        '200':
+          description: Send status
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: '#/components/schemas/ActionStatus'
+  /cloud/storage/upload:
+    post:
+      operationId: storage_upload
+      summary: Upload a file to cloud storage (Drive/Yandex/Dropsbox)

Spelling error in the summary: "Dropsbox" should be "Dropbox".
```suggestion
      summary: Upload a file to cloud storage (Drive/Yandex/Dropbox)
```

> +      required: [device_id, payload]
+      properties:
+        device_id:
+          type: string

The ConfigureDeviceRequest schema has a "device_id" field that is already provided in the path parameter. This creates redundancy as the device_id would be specified both in the URL path and in the request body. Consider removing the device_id field from the request body schema to avoid potential inconsistencies.
```suggestion
      required: [payload]
      properties:
```

> +      required: [device_id, command]
+      properties:
+        device_id:
+          type: string

The RunCommandRequest includes a "device_id" field that is redundant. The command endpoint path is "/network/command" (not parameterized by device_id), but the device_id should either be in the path or in the body. For REST consistency, when a resource is being acted upon, the ID should typically be in the path. Consider either: (1) moving device_id to a path parameter with a route like "/network/devices/{device_id}/command", or (2) clarifying in the API description that this endpoint supports multi-device command execution if that's the intent.
```suggestion
      required: [device_ids, command]
      properties:
        device_ids:
          type: array
          description: List of device IDs to execute the command against. Supports single- or multi-device command execution.
          items:
            type: string
```

> +  /network/command:
+    post:
+      operationId: run_command
+      summary: Run a command on a target device (SSH/WinRM/API)
+      tags: [network]
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: '#/components/schemas/RunCommandRequest'
+      responses:
+        '200':
+          description: Command execution result
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: '#/components/schemas/CommandResult'

Security concern: The API endpoints for destructive operations (reboot_device, remove_device, run_command) lack explicit input validation or security controls in the OpenAPI specification. While the VASER_SUPER_ADMIN_ROLE.md mentions approval requirements, these should be documented in the OpenAPI spec itself using security schemes or operation-level security requirements. Consider adding an x-requires-approval extension or security scheme to make these requirements machine-readable.

> +    LocalRunRequest:
+      type: object
+      required: [command]
+      properties:
+        command:
+          type: string
+        cwd:
+          type: string
+        timeout_seconds:
+          type: integer
+          minimum: 1
+          default: 120
+      additionalProperties: false

Security concern: The LocalRunRequest allows execution of arbitrary commands with configurable working directory and timeout, but lacks any constraints or validation requirements in the schema. This is a critical security risk. Consider adding: (1) an allowlist pattern in the description, (2) a required "reason" or "justification" field for audit purposes, or (3) validation patterns to prevent command injection attacks.

--

original.subject: Re: [Unified-system-Core/Unified_System_Core] Add VASER Super Admin planning docs and OpenAPI manifest (PR #35)
original.body:
@Copilot commented on this pull request.

## Pull request overview

Adds planning documentation and a single OpenAPI 3.1 manifest intended to standardize “VASER Super Admin” GPT action routing through the VASER Control Hub.

**Changes:**
- Introduces an OpenAPI 3.1 manifest defining hub-routed actions across network, Home Assistant, local, cloud, management, analytics, and presentations.
- Adds role/confirmation policy and safety constraints documentation for the Super Admin persona.
- Documents the Control Hub architecture plus a productization/presentation plan and sample presentation JSON outline.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 6 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| docs/vaser/super_admin_openapi.yaml | Adds the OpenAPI manifest describing the Super Admin Hub API surface (paths + schemas). |
| docs/vaser/super_admin_role.md | Defines the Super Admin role, confirmation matrix, and safety/backup rules. |
| docs/vaser/vaser_hub_architecture.md | Provides a high-level hub component breakdown and mermaid sequence flow. |
| docs/vaser/product_plan_and_presentation.md | Outlines MVP→pilot→product roadmap and a JSON outline for presentation generation. |





> +      properties:
+        path:
+          type: string
+        contents:
+          type: string

`LocalWriteRequest.path` + `contents` allows writing to any path. Consider constraining writes to an allowlisted workspace/root (and documenting that enforcement), or requiring a scoped destination reference so the manifest doesn’t imply arbitrary filesystem write capability.

> +   - Two sites: home lab + cloud tenant.
+   - Validate SSH/WinRM/API runners.
+   - Collect user feedback and logs.
+3. **Reliability & Security Hardening**
+   - Audit log integrity.
+   - Role-based access control.
+   - Automated backup and rollback.
+4. **Marketplace Integrations**
+   - Google Calendar, Gmail/Outlook, Drive, Dropbox, Yandex Disk.
+   - Home Assistant service adapters.
+5. **Scale & Operations**
+   - Multi-tenant hub with isolated vaults.
+   - SLA monitoring and incident response.
+   - Billing and usage analytics.
+
+## Presentation Structure (Generate_Presentation Input Guide)

The section title uses `Generate_Presentation`, but the OpenAPI `operationId` is `generate_presentation`. Using the exact operationId in this heading would reduce ambiguity for readers wiring the JSON outline into the action.
```suggestion
## Presentation Structure (generate_presentation Input Guide)
```

> +    DeviceSummary:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        device_id:
+          type: string

`DeviceSummary` has no `required` fields, so an empty object would validate inside `NetworkScanResponse.devices`. Consider marking the key identifiers/status fields as required (or adding `minProperties`) so clients can rely on the scan output shape.

> +      type: object
+      properties:
+        device_id:
+          type: string
+        ip_address:
+          type: string
+      required: [device_id]

`DeviceInfoRequest` includes both `device_id` and `ip_address` but the schema requires only `device_id`, making `ip_address` unusable. Either remove `ip_address` or change the schema to allow lookup by either field (e.g., `oneOf` with mutually exclusive required sets).
```suggestion
      oneOf:
        - type: object
          properties:
            device_id:
              type: string
          required: [device_id]
        - type: object
          properties:
            ip_address:
              type: string
          required: [ip_address]
```

> +      responses:
+        '200':
+          description: Discovered devices summary.
+          content:
+            application/json:

The operation documents only a `200` response. Since the API is secured (bearerAuth) and will reject invalid requests, add standard error responses (e.g., 400/401/403/404/409/500) and a shared error schema so clients/GPT actions can handle failures consistently.

> +      type: object
+      properties:
+        path:
+          type: string
+      required: [path]

`LocalReadRequest.path` is an unconstrained filesystem path, which (if implemented literally) enables arbitrary file reads and increases secret exfiltration risk. To align with the safety constraints, document enforced allowlisted roots / traversal rejection, or model the request using a scoped file reference instead of a raw path.

--

original.subject: Re: [Unified-system-Core/Unified_System_Core] Add VASER Super-Admin OpenAPI spec (PR #31)
original.body:
@Copilot commented on this pull request.

## Pull request overview

This PR adds a comprehensive OpenAPI 3.1.0 specification for Super-Admin actions routed through the VASER Router, enabling Custom GPT actions and integration with the VASER control hub. The specification defines consistent security/authorization headers and covers all required action groups including network operations, Home Assistant integration, local gateway operations, cloud integrations (Google Calendar, iCloud, Gmail, Outlook, Google Drive, Yandex Disk, Dropbox), management operations, and analytics workflows.

**Changes:**
- Added `docs/specs/super-admin-openapi.yaml` with 1389 lines defining 34 API endpoints across 6 functional areas (Network, HomeAssistant, Local, Cloud, Management, Analytics)
- Updated `Agent_Context/Knowledge_Base/Docs/VASYA_GATEWAY.md` to include a reference to the new OpenAPI specification

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 24 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| docs/specs/super-admin-openapi.yaml | Complete OpenAPI specification defining all Super-Admin endpoints, security schemes, and request/response schemas for the VASER Router API |
| Agent_Context/Knowledge_Base/Docs/VASYA_GATEWAY.md | Added documentation section linking to the new OpenAPI spec |

            validated and escaped by the backend before execution.

            type: string
        cwd:
          type: string
          description: >
            Optional working directory for the process. The backend may
            restrict this to a set of safe directories and will reject
            invalid or unauthorized paths.

          type: integer
          description: >
            Optional maximum execution time in seconds. The backend
            enforces an upper bound and will terminate commands that
            exceed allowed limits.
