+      type: object
+      properties:
+        start:
+          type: string
+          format: date-time
+        end:
+          type: string
+          format: date-time

The TimeRange schema does not validate that 'end' is after 'start', which could lead to invalid time ranges being accepted. While this validation might be performed server-side, documenting it in the OpenAPI spec would help API consumers understand the constraints. Consider adding a description noting this requirement or using OpenAPI extensions to express this constraint.

> +          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: '#/components/schemas/TaskReminderRequest'
+      responses:
+        '200':
+          description: Reminder status
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: '#/components/schemas/TaskResponse'
+  /reports/generate:
+    post:
+      summary: Generate a management report
+      operationId: generate_report
+      tags: [Management]
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: '#/components/schemas/ReportRequest'
+      responses:
+        '200':
+          description: Report status
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: '#/components/schemas/ReportResponse'
+  /analytics/collect-logs:
+    post:
+      summary: Collect logs from managed systems
+      operationId: collect_logs
+      tags: [Analytics]
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: '#/components/schemas/LogCollectionRequest'
+      responses:
+        '200':
+          description: Log collection result
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LogCollectionResponse'
+  /analytics/analyze-logs:
+    post:
+      summary: Analyze logs for issues
+      operationId: analyze_logs
+      tags: [Analytics]
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: '#/components/schemas/LogAnalysisRequest'
+      responses:
+        '200':
+          description: Analysis output
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LogAnalysisResponse'
+  /analytics/summarize:
+    post:
+      summary: Summarize project state
+      operationId: summarize_project
+      tags: [Analytics]
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: '#/components/schemas/ProjectSummaryRequest'
+      responses:
+        '200':
+          description: Summary output
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: '#/components/schemas/ProjectSummaryResponse'
+  /analytics/presentation:
+    post:
+      summary: Generate presentation from content JSON
+      operationId: generate_presentation
+      tags: [Analytics]
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: '#/components/schemas/PresentationRequest'
+      responses:
+        '200':
+          description: Presentation output metadata
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: '#/components/schemas/PresentationResponse'

All API endpoints only define a 200 success response but are missing critical error response codes. Production APIs should document error responses including 400 (Bad Request), 401 (Unauthorized), 403 (Forbidden), 404 (Not Found), 422 (Unprocessable Entity), 429 (Rate Limit), and 500 (Internal Server Error). This is especially important for security-sensitive operations like device command execution and Home Assistant control. Without documented error responses, API consumers cannot properly handle failures.

> +    All endpoints are executed by VASER-Hub which enforces policy, auditing,
+    and confirmation gates before performing device or cloud actions.
+servers:
+  - url: https://vaser-hub.local/api
+    description: Internal VASER Control Hub
+  - url: https://{region}.vaser-hub.net/api
+    description: Managed VASER Cloud Hub
+    variables:
+      region:
+        default: eu
+        enum:
+          - eu
+          - us
+security:
+  - bearerAuth: []
+  - apiKeyAuth: []

The security scheme at the global level uses OR logic between bearerAuth and apiKeyAuth (both are in an array with OR semantics in OpenAPI 3.0). This means clients can choose either authentication method. For a Super Admin API with destructive capabilities, you should require both authentication methods (AND logic) or explicitly document which operations require which authentication. Consider using security at the operation level to enforce stricter authentication requirements for high-risk operations like reboot_device, remove_device, and configure_device.
```suggestion
    apiKeyAuth: []
```

> +    LocalWriteRequest:
+      type: object
+      required: [path, content]
+      properties:
+        path:
+          type: string
+        content:
+          type: string
+        mode:
+          type: string
+          enum: [overwrite, append]
+          default: overwrite
+        require_confirmation:
+          type: boolean
+          default: true

The LocalWriteRequest schema lacks critical validation and security controls. The 'path' field accepts any string without restrictions, which could allow path traversal attacks (e.g., "../../etc/passwd"). There should be validation to ensure paths are within allowed directories, maximum path length constraints, and filename validation. Additionally, the 'content' field has no size limits, which could enable denial-of-service attacks through excessive file writes.

> +          type: object
+          additionalProperties: true

The HaServiceCallRequest schema has a completely open 'data' object (additionalProperties: true) without any validation. While Home Assistant services do require flexible data payloads, this could enable injection attacks or abuse. Consider adding documentation or examples of expected data structures for common safety-critical services mentioned in vaser-super-admin-role.md (locks, alarms, power, HVAC), and consider implementing server-side validation for these critical entity types.
```suggestion
          $ref: '#/components/schemas/HaServiceCallData'
    HaServiceCallData:
      type: object
      description: |
        Service-specific payload for Home Assistant service calls.

        This object is intentionally flexible to support the wide variety of
        Home Assistant services. For non-safety-critical services, arbitrary
        key/value pairs may be used as documented by the corresponding
        Home Assistant integration.

        For safety-critical services (e.g. locks, alarms, power, HVAC) invoked
        via this API and described in vaser-super-admin-role.md, server-side
        validation MUST enforce service-specific schemas. Clients SHOULD only
        use documented fields for those services. Examples:

          - Lock service:
              service: lock.lock
              entity_id: lock.front_door
              data:
                code: "1234"

          - HVAC service:
              service: climate.set_temperature
              entity_id: climate.living_room
              data:
                temperature: 21.5
                hvac_mode: "heat"

      additionalProperties:
        oneOf:
          - type: string
          - type: number
          - type: boolean
          - type: array
          - type: object
```

-- 
Reply to this email directly or view it on GitHub:
https://github.com/Unified-system-Core/Unified_System_Core/pull/5#pullrequestreview-3703919950
You are receiving this because you modified the open/close state.

Message ID: <Unified-system-Core/Unified_System_Core/pull/5/review/3703919950@github.com>

analysis.subject: Documentation Update for VASER Super Admin Platform
analysis.reply_body:
This PR introduces comprehensive documentation for the VASER Super Admin platform, including an OpenAPI specification for a unified API that mediates high-privilege operations across networks, Home Assistant, local gateways, and cloud services. The documentation establishes a security-first architecture with policy enforcement, confirmation workflows, and audit logging as core requirements.

**Changes:**
- Added OpenAPI 3.0 specification defining 23 endpoints across Network, Home Assistant, Local, Cloud, Management, and Analytics domains
- Created security analysis document outlining risk areas, required controls (MFA, short-lived tokens, confirmation gates), and command safety policy
- Documented Super Admin role instructions with mandatory safety workflow and prohibited behaviors
- Provided architecture overview describing VASER-Hub components (API Gateway, Policy Engine, Vault, Orchestrator, Audit Logger)
- Outlined product roadmap with 3-phase plan spanning 6 months

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 24 comments.
analysis.reason: This email details a significant update regarding documentation that is relevant for stakeholders and team members involved in the VASER Super Admin project.

strategy.plan: Send the documentation update to the relevant stakeholders via email to ensure they are informed about new changes and can review the updates accordingly.
strategy.roi: This action ensures that all team members are aligned on the recent developments, which may lead to improved collaboration and project outcomes.

original.subject: Re: [Unified-system-Core/Unified_System_Core] Add Super Admin OpenAPI spec (VASER Control Hub) (PR #6)
original.body:
@Copilot commented on this pull request.

## Pull request overview

This PR adds a comprehensive OpenAPI 3.1 specification for the VASER Control Hub Super Admin API, designed to integrate with a Custom GPT. The specification defines 30+ endpoints across six functional domains (Network, Home Assistant, Local Gateway, Clouds, Management, and Analytics) with standardized authentication, request/response schemas, and error handling following the Vasya Gateway conventions.

**Changes:**
- Added `docs/openapi/super_admin.yaml` with complete API specification for Super Admin GPT actions
- Defined security schemes for bearer tokens, API keys, and OAuth2 cloud provider integrations
- Specified comprehensive request/response schemas for network management, home automation, local command execution, cloud integrations, task management, and analytics operations





> +          type: string
+        status:
+          type: string
+          enum: [online, offline, unknown]
+        vendor:
+          type: string
+      required: [device_id, ip, status]
+    DeviceInfoRequest:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        device_id:
+          type: string
+        ip:
+          type: string
+          format: ipv4
+      oneOf:

The `oneOf` keyword is placed at the same level as `properties` in the schema, but it should be used to define mutually exclusive schema alternatives. The current structure is incorrect because it attempts to combine properties with oneOf requirements. Either use separate schema definitions with oneOf at the top level, or restructure to require at least one of device_id or ip without using oneOf.
```suggestion
      anyOf:
```

> +          items:
+            type: string
+      required: [hostname, ip]
+    RemoveDeviceRequest:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        device_id:
+          type: string
+        ip:
+          type: string
+          format: ipv4
+        confirm:
+          type: boolean
+          description: Required for critical deletions.
+          default: false
+      required: [confirm]

The RemoveDeviceRequest schema only requires `confirm` but does not require any device identifier (device_id or ip). This makes it impossible to specify which device to remove. At least one of device_id or ip should be required to identify the target device.
```suggestion
      required: [device_id, confirm]
```

> +          description: Required for critical deletions.
+          default: false
+      required: [confirm]
+    RebootDeviceRequest:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        device_id:
+          type: string
+        ip:
+          type: string
+          format: ipv4
+        confirm:
+          type: boolean
+          description: Required for reboot of critical devices.
+          default: false
+      required: [confirm]

The RebootDeviceRequest schema only requires `confirm` but does not require any device identifier (device_id or ip). This makes it impossible to specify which device to reboot. At least one of device_id or ip should be required to identify the target device.
```suggestion
      required: [confirm]
      anyOf:
        - required: [device_id]
        - required: [ip]
```

> +security:
+  - bearerAuth: []
+  - apiKeyAuth: []
+  - oauth2Cloud: []

The global security array lists all three authentication schemes (bearerAuth, apiKeyAuth, oauth2Cloud) at the root level, which means clients would need to satisfy all three schemes simultaneously. This is likely not the intended behavior. Security schemes should typically be alternatives (OR) rather than requirements (AND). Individual endpoints override this with their own security requirements, but the global default is semantically incorrect for an API where these are alternative authentication methods.
```suggestion
security: []
```

> +    patch:
+      tags: [Management]
+      summary: complete_task
+      description: Mark a task as completed.
+      operationId: complete_task
+      security:
+        - bearerAuth: []
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: "#/components/schemas/CompleteTaskRequest"
+      responses:
+        "200":
+          description: Task updated.
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: "#/components/schemas/TaskResponse"
+        "400": { $ref: "#/components/responses/BadRequest" }
+        "401": { $ref: "#/components/responses/Unauthorized" }
+        "404": { $ref: "#/components/responses/NotFound" }
+        "500": { $ref: "#/components/responses/InternalError" }

Using PATCH on /management/task for task completion is RESTful, but the endpoint doesn't include a task identifier in the path. The typical REST pattern would be PATCH /management/task/{taskId} rather than PATCH /management/task with task_id in the body. This makes the API less predictable and harder to use with standard REST tooling. Consider restructuring to use path parameters for resource identifiers.

> +  /ha/get_state:
+    post:
+      tags: [Home Assistant]
+      summary: ha.get_state
+      description: Get state of a Home Assistant entity.
+      operationId: ha_get_state
+      security:
+        - bearerAuth: []
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: "#/components/schemas/HaGetStateRequest"
+      responses:
+        "200":
+          description: Entity state.
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: "#/components/schemas/HaGetStateResponse"
+        "400": { $ref: "#/components/responses/BadRequest" }
+        "401": { $ref: "#/components/responses/Unauthorized" }
+        "404": { $ref: "#/components/responses/NotFound" }
+        "500": { $ref: "#/components/responses/InternalError" }

The /ha/get_state endpoint uses POST method for a read operation. REST conventions suggest GET should be used for retrieving data without side effects. While POST may be intentional for consistency with a GPT Actions pattern or to avoid exposing entity_id in URLs, this deviates from standard RESTful practice. Consider either using GET /ha/state/{entityId} or documenting the rationale for this design choice.

> +  /local/run:
+    post:
+      tags: [Local Gateway]
+      summary: /local/run
+      description: Execute a local command using the Python gateway.
+      operationId: local_run
+      security:
+        - bearerAuth: []
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: "#/components/schemas/LocalRunRequest"
+      responses:
+        "200":
+          description: Command execution output.
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: "#/components/schemas/LocalRunResponse"
+        "400": { $ref: "#/components/responses/BadRequest" }
+        "401": { $ref: "#/components/responses/Unauthorized" }
+        "500": { $ref: "#/components/responses/InternalError" }
+  /local/read:
+    post:
+      tags: [Local Gateway]
+      summary: /local/read
+      description: Read a file from the local gateway sandbox.
+      operationId: local_read
+      security:
+        - bearerAuth: []
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: "#/components/schemas/LocalReadRequest"
+      responses:
+        "200":
+          description: File contents.
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: "#/components/schemas/LocalReadResponse"
+        "400": { $ref: "#/components/responses/BadRequest" }
+        "401": { $ref: "#/components/responses/Unauthorized" }
+        "404": { $ref: "#/components/responses/NotFound" }
+        "500": { $ref: "#/components/responses/InternalError" }
+  /local/write:
+    post:
+      tags: [Local Gateway]
+      summary: /local/write
+      description: Write a file through the local gateway sandbox.
+      operationId: local_write
+      security:
+        - bearerAuth: []
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: "#/components/schemas/LocalWriteRequest"
+      responses:
+        "200":
+          description: Write confirmation.
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: "#/components/schemas/LocalWriteResponse"
+        "400": { $ref: "#/components/responses/BadRequest" }
+        "401": { $ref: "#/components/responses/Unauthorized" }
+        "500": { $ref: "#/components/responses/InternalError" }

The /local/run, /local/read, and /local/write endpoints allow arbitrary command execution and file system access but only return 401 (Unauthorized) and 500 (InternalError) responses. They're missing 403 (Forbidden) responses for authorization/permission failures. These are high-risk operations that should have explicit authorization checks documented in the API spec.

> +    LocalReadRequest:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        path:
+          type: string
+      required: [path]
+    LocalReadResponse:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        path:
+          type: string
+        content:
+          type: string
+        encoding:
+          type: string
+          default: utf-8
+      required: [path, content]
+    LocalWriteRequest:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        path:
+          type: string
+        content:
+          type: string
+        encoding:
+          type: string
+          default: utf-8
+        mode:
+          type: string
+          enum: [overwrite, append]
+          default: overwrite
+      required: [path, content]

The LocalReadRequest and LocalWriteRequest schemas accept arbitrary file paths without any documented constraints or patterns. This is a security concern as it could allow reading/writing any file the service account has access to. While the description mentions a "sandbox", the schema provides no validation boundaries. Consider adding: 1) pattern constraints (e.g., must start with a safe prefix), 2) explicit documentation about path restrictions, or 3) examples showing valid sandbox paths.

> +      summary: /local/run
+      description: Execute a local command using the Python gateway.
+      operationId: local_run
+      security:
+        - bearerAuth: []
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: "#/components/schemas/LocalRunRequest"
+      responses:
+        "200":
+          description: Command execution output.
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: "#/components/schemas/LocalRunResponse"
+        "400": { $ref: "#/components/responses/BadRequest" }
+        "401": { $ref: "#/components/responses/Unauthorized" }
+        "500": { $ref: "#/components/responses/InternalError" }
+  /local/read:
+    post:
+      tags: [Local Gateway]
+      summary: /local/read
+      description: Read a file from the local gateway sandbox.
+      operationId: local_read
+      security:
+        - bearerAuth: []
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: "#/components/schemas/LocalReadRequest"
+      responses:
+        "200":
+          description: File contents.
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: "#/components/schemas/LocalReadResponse"
+        "400": { $ref: "#/components/responses/BadRequest" }
+        "401": { $ref: "#/components/responses/Unauthorized" }
+        "404": { $ref: "#/components/responses/NotFound" }
+        "500": { $ref: "#/components/responses/InternalError" }
+  /local/write:
+    post:
+      tags: [Local Gateway]
+      summary: /local/write

The summary field for Local Gateway endpoints uses the path itself (e.g., "/local/run", "/local/read", "/local/write") rather than a descriptive action name. This is inconsistent with other endpoints which use action-oriented summaries like "scan_network", "get_device_info", or "run_command". Consider using summaries like "execute_local_command", "read_local_file", and "write_local_file" for consistency.

> +    delete:
+      tags: [Network]
+      summary: remove_device
+      description: Remove a device from VASER Hub.
+      operationId: remove_device
+      security:
+        - bearerAuth: []
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: "#/components/schemas/RemoveDeviceRequest"

The DELETE operation includes a required request body. While this is technically allowed in HTTP and OpenAPI, it's not a common RESTful pattern and may cause issues with some HTTP clients and API gateways that don't support request bodies in DELETE requests. Consider moving the device identifier to the URL path (e.g., DELETE /network/device/{deviceId}) or using query parameters, with the confirm flag as an optional query parameter or header.

> +        message:
+          type: string
+      required: [status]
+    CloudStorageRequest:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        action:
+          type: string
+          enum: [list, upload, download, delete]
+        path:
+          type: string
+        destination_path:
+          type: string
+        metadata:
+          type: object
+      required: [action]

The CloudStorageRequest schema only requires 'action' but actions like 'upload', 'download', and 'delete' logically require a 'path' parameter to specify which file to operate on. The 'list' action might work without a path (to list root), but the other actions will fail. Consider using oneOf to specify different requirements for different actions, or add conditional validation documentation.
```suggestion
      required: [action]
      oneOf:
        - properties:
            action:
              const: list
          description: List files; path is optional and, if omitted, lists the root.
        - properties:
            action:
              const: upload
          required: [action, path]
        - properties:
            action:
              const: download
          required: [action, path]
        - properties:
            action:
              const: delete
          required: [action, path]
```

> +        bytes_written:
+          type: integer
+      required: [path, bytes_written]
+    CloudCalendarRequest:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        action:
+          type: string
+          enum: [create, update, delete, list]
+        calendar_id:
+          type: string
+        event:
+          $ref: "#/components/schemas/CalendarEvent"
+        query:
+          type: object
+      required: [action]

The CloudCalendarRequest schema only requires 'action', but actions like 'create', 'update', and 'delete' logically require an 'event' parameter or event ID. The 'create' and 'update' actions need event details, while 'delete' and 'update' need an event identifier. Consider using oneOf to specify different requirements for different actions, or add conditional validation documentation.
```suggestion
      required: [action]
      oneOf:
        - properties:
            action:
              const: create
          required: [action, calendar_id, event]
        - properties:
            action:
              const: update
          required: [action, calendar_id, event]
        - properties:
            action:
              const: delete
          required: [action, calendar_id, event]
        - properties:
            action:
              const: list
          required: [action, calendar_id]
```

> +    CloudMailRequest:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        provider:
+          type: string
+          enum: [gmail, outlook]
+        action:
+          type: string
+          enum: [send, list, read, delete]
+        message:
+          $ref: "#/components/schemas/MailMessage"
+        query:
+          type: object
+      required: [provider, action]
+    CloudMailResponse:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        status:
+          type: string
+        messages:
+          type: array
+          items:
+            $ref: "#/components/schemas/MailMessage"
+        message:
+          type: string
+      required: [status]

The CloudMailRequest defines 'message' (singular) property that references MailMessage schema, but this is confusing because the response uses both 'messages' (plural, array) and 'message' (singular, string for status). For the 'send' action, a message is required, but for 'list', 'read', 'delete', it's unclear how to specify which message to operate on. Consider: 1) renaming to 'mail_message' or 'email' to avoid confusion with status messages, 2) adding a 'message_id' field for operations on existing messages.

> +          format: ipv4
+        protocol:
+          type: string
+          enum: [ssh, winrm, api]
+        command:
+          type: string
+        confirm:
+          type: boolean
+          description: Whether user confirmation has been obtained for critical commands.
+          default: false
+        timeout_seconds:
+          type: integer
+          minimum: 1
+          maximum: 3600
+          default: 60
+      required: [protocol, command]

The RunCommandRequest schema does not require any device identifier (device_id or ip) in the required array, even though the operation needs to target a specific device. While protocol and command are required, at least one of device_id or ip should also be required to identify where to run the command.
```suggestion
      required: [protocol, command]
      anyOf:
        - required: [device_id]
        - required: [ip]
```

> +      description: OAuth2 tokens for cloud providers (Google, Apple, Microsoft, Yandex, Dropbox).
+      flows:
+        authorizationCode:
+          authorizationUrl: https://auth.vaser-hub.example.com/oauth/authorize
+          tokenUrl: https://auth.vaser-hub.example.com/oauth/token
+          scopes:
+            calendar: Access calendar data
+            mail: Access mail data
+            drive: Access drive files
+            icloud: Access iCloud data
+            disk: Access Yandex Disk data
+            dropbox: Access Dropbox data

The OAuth2 security scheme uses generic authorization and token URLs (auth.vaser-hub.example.com) and defines scopes that mix different cloud providers (Google, iCloud, Yandex, Dropbox) under one scheme. However, each cloud provider has its own OAuth2 flows with different authorization URLs and token endpoints. This design will not work in practice - you should either: 1) Define separate OAuth2 security schemes for each provider (googleOAuth2, icloudOAuth2, etc.) with their respective URLs, or 2) Document that this is a proxy/gateway pattern where VASER Hub manages the provider-specific OAuth flows.
```suggestion
      description: >
        OAuth2 tokens issued by the VASER Hub auth gateway, which proxies and
        manages provider-specific OAuth flows for cloud services (Google, Apple,
        Microsoft, Yandex, Dropbox). These scopes are logical VASER Hub scopes,
        not the raw third-party provider scopes.
      flows:
        authorizationCode:
          authorizationUrl: https://auth.vaser-hub.example.com/oauth/authorize
          tokenUrl: https://auth.vaser-hub.example.com/oauth/token
          scopes:
            calendar: Access calendar data via VASER Hub (e.g., Google, Microsoft, Apple)
            mail: Access mail data via VASER Hub (e.g., Google, Microsoft)
            drive: Access drive files via VASER Hub (e.g., Google Drive, OneDrive)
            icloud: Access iCloud data via VASER Hub
            disk: Access Yandex Disk data via VASER Hub
            dropbox: Access Dropbox data via VASER Hub
```

> +  /network/device/info:
+    post:
+      tags: [Network]
+      summary: get_device_info
+      description: Retrieve detailed information about a device by ID or IP.
+      operationId: get_device_info
+      security:
+        - bearerAuth: []
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: "#/components/schemas/DeviceInfoRequest"
+      responses:
+        "200":
+          description: Device details.
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: "#/components/schemas/DeviceInfoResponse"
+        "400": { $ref: "#/components/responses/BadRequest" }
+        "401": { $ref: "#/components/responses/Unauthorized" }
+        "404": { $ref: "#/components/responses/NotFound" }
+        "500": { $ref: "#/components/responses/InternalError" }

The /network/device/info endpoint uses POST method for a read operation. REST conventions suggest GET should be used for retrieving data without side effects. While POST may be intentional for consistency with a GPT Actions pattern that requires all operations to be POST, this deviates from standard RESTful practice. Consider either using GET /network/device/{deviceId} or GET /network/device?ip={ip}, or documenting the rationale for this design choice.

> +    LocalRunRequest:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        command:
+          type: string
+        cwd:
+          type: string
+        timeout_seconds:
+          type: integer
+          minimum: 1
+          maximum: 3600
+          default: 120
+        env:
+          type: object
+          additionalProperties:
+            type: string
+      required: [command]

The LocalRunRequest schema accepts an arbitrary command string without any documented constraints, patterns, or examples. This is a critical security concern as it allows unrestricted command execution. While the description mentions a "Python gateway" and "sandbox", the schema itself provides no validation or security boundaries. Consider adding: 1) pattern constraints or enum for allowed commands, 2) a note about server-side validation and sandboxing, or 3) explicit documentation about which commands are allowed/blocked.

--

original.subject: Re: [Unified-system-Core/Unified_System_Core] Add local reminder action to OpenAPI and expand VASER-Hub architecture & product plan (PR #10)
original.body:
@Copilot commented on this pull request.

## Pull request overview

This PR adds a local reminder scheduling capability to the VASER Super Admin system and expands the architecture documentation with detailed component descriptions and a product roadmap.

**Changes:**
- Added `/local/reminder` POST endpoint to the OpenAPI specification for scheduling reminders through the local gateway
- Documented VASER-Hub's internal architecture with seven key components (API Gateway, Policy Engine, Credentials Vault, Connectors Layer, Job Queue + Orchestrator, Audit Log, Metrics & Health)
- Added a four-phase product rollout plan (MVP → Beta → v1.0 → Enterprise) with timeline estimates

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| Agent_Context/Knowledge_Base/Docs/VASER_SUPER_ADMIN_OPENAPI.yaml | Adds `/local/reminder` endpoint with LocalReminderRequest and LocalReminderResponse schemas |
| Agent_Context/Knowledge_Base/Docs/VASER_SUPER_ADMIN_BRIEF.md | Expands architecture section with component breakdown, trust model, and adds product rollout roadmap |





> +      type: object
+      required: [message, remind_at]
+      properties:
+        message:
+          type: string
+        remind_at:
+          type: string
+          format: date-time
+        metadata:
+          type: object
+    LocalReminderResponse:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        reminder_id:
+          type: string
+        status:
+          type: string
     CloudCalendarRequest:
       type: object

The LocalReminderRequest and LocalReminderResponse schemas are nearly identical to the existing ReminderRequest and ReminderResponse schemas (lines 905-920 in the components section). This creates unnecessary duplication in the API specification. Consider reusing the existing ReminderRequest and ReminderResponse schemas instead of creating new ones, or if there are differences that justify separate schemas, document what those differences are.
```suggestion
      $ref: '#/components/schemas/ReminderRequest'
    LocalReminderResponse:
      $ref: '#/components/schemas/ReminderResponse'
    CloudCalendarRequest:
      type: object
      required: [provider, action]
      properties:
        provider:
          type: string
          enum: [google, icloud]
        action:
          type: string
          enum: [list, create, update, delete]
        payload:
          type: object
      type: object
```

> @@ -781,6 +799,24 @@ components:
           type: string
         bytes_written:
           type: integer
+    LocalReminderRequest:
+      type: object
+      required: [message, remind_at]
+      properties:
+        message:
+          type: string
+        remind_at:
+          type: string
+          format: date-time
+        metadata:
+          type: object

The metadata field lacks a description explaining its purpose and expected structure. While other fields in the API specification generally have minimal descriptions, adding a description here would clarify what metadata should be included (e.g., tags, priority, recurring settings). Consider adding a description field to explain the intended use of this optional metadata object.
```suggestion
          type: object
          description: Optional free-form metadata for the reminder, such as tags, priority, or recurrence settings.
```

> +      required: [message, remind_at]
+      properties:
+        message:
+          type: string
+        remind_at:
+          type: string
+          format: date-time
+        metadata:
+          type: object
+    LocalReminderResponse:
+      type: object
+      properties:
+        reminder_id:
+          type: string
+        status:
+          type: string

The status field should define allowed enum values to make the API contract explicit. Looking at similar endpoints in the specification (e.g., DeviceSummary.status with enum values 'online', 'offline', 'unknown'), the status field should specify what values are valid such as 'scheduled', 'pending', 'failed', or other appropriate states for a reminder.
```suggestion
          type: string
          enum: [scheduled, pending, completed, failed, cancelled]
```

> +  /local/reminder:
+    post:
+      tags: [Local]
+      summary: Schedule a local reminder.
+      operationId: local.reminder
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: '#/components/schemas/LocalReminderRequest'
+      responses:
+        '200':
+          description: Reminder scheduled.
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LocalReminderResponse'

The addition of /local/reminder creates potential confusion with the existing /management/reminder endpoint (line 409). Both endpoints appear to serve the same purpose of scheduling reminders, but are under different tags (Local vs Management). The PR description states this is for "scheduling reminders via the local gateway," but it's unclear what distinguishes a "local" reminder from a "management" reminder. Consider clarifying in the summary or operationId description when to use /local/reminder versus /management/reminder, or consolidate these endpoints if they serve the same purpose.

--

original.subject: חשבנו עליך כשראינו את המשרה הזו: דרוש /ה דרוש מהנדס ביצוע לפרטים נוספים >>
original.body:
איגור שלום,
ברגעים אלו מעסיק העלה משרה חמה שנמצאה מתאימה להגדרות החיפוש שלך.
עדכון על כל משרה חמה שעולה לאתר מאפשר לך להיות הראשון לשלוח קורות חיים ובכך להגדיל את סכוייך למצוא עבודה.
דרוש /ה דרוש מהנדס ביצוע
חברה: נדל"ן / בינוי / תשתיות
מקום העבודה: מספר אזורים
סוג משרה: משרה מלאה
לחברת ביצוע ויזמות באזור המרכז דרוש/ה דרוש מהנדס ביצוע (לא הנדסאי!)


עם ניסיון של מינימום 3 שנים בחברה קבלנית עם סיווג...
לצפייה במשרה
תזמון דיוורים הגדרות חיפוש צור קשר 077-6-000-000
להסרה מרשימת תפוצה לחץ כאן
זוהי פניה בדיוור ישיר בהתאם למאגר רשום בבעלות אול יו ניד בע"מ ("החברה"), שמספרו 700013539 אשר מטרתו הינה, בין היתר, לצרכי דיוור ישיר. המידע במאגר התקבל ממך במסגרת הצטרפותך ו/או חברותך ו/או הירשמותך לקבל את שירותי החברה באתר האינטרנט (כהגדרתו מטה). באפשרותך לבקש הסרתך מהמאגר על-ידי פנייה בהודעה בכתב למשרדי החברה בכתובת בני ברמן 2, מגדל עיר ימים, נתניה 4249330 או באמצעות מתן הודעה באתר האינטרנט של החברה בכתובת האינטרנט www.alljobs.co.il . ניתן גם לפנות אלינו בכתובת הדואר האלקטרוני nituk@alljobs.co.il או בטלפון 077-6000000. החברה תפעל לטפל בבקשתך בהתאם למועדים הקבועים בחוק הגנת הפרטיות, תשמ"א-1981.
ליצירת קשר לחץ כאן.

Here are a few openings:

1. **Operations Managers (Remote)** at Keystone Recruitment - [View Job](https://www.linkedin.com/comm/jobs/view/4365674299/?trackingId=uidfwfyo6W7dNYoTmVBlfA%3D%3D)

3. **Project Associate** at HasoubLabs, Arara - [View Job](https://www.linkedin.com/comm/jobs/view/4355741180/?trackingId=K4ckUME9zEAlg785RqLRbg%3D%3D)

4. **ICT and JTAG Manager** at NVIDIA, Raanana - [View Job](https://www.linkedin.com/comm/jobs/view/4333147873/?trackingId=GKGy3RoASGSmicB0vtvNjA%3D%3D)

5. **Project Managers (Remote)** at Keystone Recruitment - [View Job](https://www.linkedin.com/comm/jobs/view/4365665855/?trackingId=0ByIHQyG6r10KbXOED9sEA%3D%3D)

If you're interested or know someone who might be a good fit, I encourage you to apply! 

Best regards,
[Your Name]

Igor Goncharenko
Operations Manager | Technical Project Manager | Integrator
Kiryat Bialik | 052-841-6-550 | gonya90.gg@gmail.com | LinkedIn Profile
Executive Summary
Experienced Operations and Project Manager specializing in leading
technical teams, managing complex logistics, and supervising infrastructure
projects (high-rise construction, electro-mechanical systems,
communications). Proven ability in real-time field troubleshooting,
resource management, and technological system integration. Combines a
strong technical background (Hands-on) with broad managerial vision, high
service orientation, and self-taught capabilities in Automation and AI.
Work Experience
Schindler Group | Technical Supervisor / Foreman
2023 – Present
* Project Management: Comprehensive management and supervision of elevator
installation projects in luxury high-rise towers, from planning to delivery.
* Site Operations: Operational management of the work site, including
coordination with contractors, engineering, safety teams, and schedule
management (Gantt).
* Logistics: Full responsibility for site inventory, equipment ordering,
receiving goods, and supply chain management.
* Integration: Connecting command and control systems and resolving complex
technical malfunctions in the field.
* Safety & Quality: Implementation of global safety procedures, quality
control (QA/QC), and employee training.
Partner (Subcontractor: URICOMS) | Team Leader – Infrastructure & Fiber
2019 – 2023
* Team Leadership: Managed and led technician teams in the deployment of
fiber optic infrastructure (FTTH) and communications.
* Performance Management: Responsible for meeting performance targets (KPIs
/ SLA), installation quality, and customer satisfaction.
* Training & Mentorship: Professional training of employees, implementation
of work procedures, and process improvement.
* Problem Solving: Worked in a dynamic, high-pressure environment,
providing technical solutions for infrastructure issues in residential and
business buildings.
Vira & Harkavot Ltd | Elevator & Escalator Technician
2020 – 2022
* Installation: Execution of mechanical and electrical installations (Heavy
Duty) and reading engineering blueprints.
* Teamwork: Worked in teams with a strict focus on precision and high-level
execution quality.
Education & Military Service
* Mechanical Practical Engineer / Technician: Diploma studies (3 years).
* Military Service: Israeli Air Force | Operational Driver | Rank: Staff
Sergeant.
Skills & Tools
* Languages: Hebrew (Very High / Working Proficiency), Russian (Native),
English (Good Technical).
* Technology: Linux, Proxmox, ERP, Office (Excel/Outlook), Home Assistant,
MQTT, AI tools for productivity improvement.
* Logistics: Warehouse management.
* Licenses: Driving License (Category B), Forklift (20 ton), Certification
for Working at Heights.


С уважением Игорь Гончаренко
בכבוד רב איגור גונצ׳רנקו

2. Director of Business Operations at Insait, also in the Tel Aviv District. You can apply [here](https://www.linkedin.com/comm/jobs/view/4355731112/?trackingId=uaHr8EbYY6yEy%2F1jyp2C9Q%3D%3D).

3. Chief Executive Officer for the Israeli Association of Skin Cancer and Melanoma, applying [here](https://www.linkedin.com/comm/jobs/view/4355393642/?trackingId=82ST5q6Pd3ppZ7ocShDZ1g%3D%3D).

4. Manager of Industry Relations for a leading academic institution at GalilTech. Apply [here](https://www.linkedin.com/comm/jobs/view/4355761855/?trackingId=kBmRNdB33HnbqeF08SvNfA%3D%3D).

5. Sales Manager position available with relocation at Cuvee Consulting. Apply [here](https://www.linkedin.com/comm/jobs/view/4321355855/?trackingId=8BAFHI4MuTdOogC8EOmtBw%3D%3D).

Best regards,
[Your Name]

איגור שלום,

משרה חמה!
דרוש /ה נהג 12 טון לחברה מובילה בכרמיאל- 9,500 ש"ח נטו + רכב צמוד!!
חברה: morejobs

סוג משרה: משרה מלאה, מתאים גם לחיילים משוחררים, מתאים גם לבני 50 פלוס, מתאים גם למגזר הדתי... 
לחברה מובילה בתחום הספקה טכנית וכלי עבודה דרוש/ה נהג/ת 12 טון (על וואן) לעבודת חלוקה.
התקפיד כולל אספקת סחורה טכנית ללקוחות...
לצפייה במשרה
משרה חמה!
רושים /ות עובדי /ות אדמיניסטרציה, בק אופיס ותומכי /ות מכירות
חברה: חן בכר

סוג משרה: משרה מלאה
לחברות מובילות בתחומים מגוונים
דרושים/ות עובדי/ות אדמיניסטרציה, בק אופיס ותמיכת מכירות לתפקידי תמיכה תפעולית ומסחרית בארגון.

עבודה...
לצפייה במשרה
משרה חמה!
מנהל /ת סניף לרשת אופנה באזור הצפון -שכר גבוה!!
חברה: Job space
מקום העבודה: מספר אזורים
סוג משרה: משרה מלאה, משמרות, מתאים גם להורים / שעות גמישות, מתאים גם לחיילים משוחררים... 
חולם/ת להוביל מותג אופנה ישראלי מצליח? אנו מחפשים מנהל /ת תותח/ית להובלת צוות וניהול סניף ברשת אופנה מובילה בצמיחה!

תיאור...
לצפייה במשרה
לצפייה בכל המשרות בפיד שלך
תזמון דיוורים הגדרות חיפוש צור קשר 077-6-000-000
להסרה מרשימת תפוצה לחץ כאן
זוהי פניה בדיוור ישיר בהתאם למאגר רשום בבעלות אול יו ניד בע"מ ("החברה"), שמספרו 700013539 אשר מטרתו הינה, בין היתר, לצרכי דיוור ישיר. המידע במאגר התקבל ממך במסגרת הצטרפותך ו/או חברותך ו/או הירשמותך לקבל את שירותי החברה באתר האינטרנט (כהגדרתו מטה). באפשרותך לבקש הסרתך מהמאגר על-ידי פנייה בהודעה בכתב למשרדי החברה בכתובת בני ברמן 2, מגדל עיר ימים, נתניה 4249330 או באמצעות מתן הודעה באתר האינטרנט של החברה בכתובת האינטרנט www.alljobs.co.il . ניתן גם לפנות אלינו בכתובת הדואר האלקטרוני nituk@alljobs.co.il או בטלפון 077-6000000. החברה תפעל לטפל בבקשתך בהתאם למועדים הקבועים בחוק הגנת הפרטיות, תשמ"א-1981.
ליצירת קשר לחץ כאן.

analysis.subject: Exciting Job Opportunities in Israel
analysis.reply_body:
Dear [Recipient],

I hope this message finds you well. I wanted to share some exciting job opportunities that match your preferences in Israel. Here are a few listings that you might find interesting:

1. **Project Associate** at HasoubLabs (Arara) - [View Job](https://www.linkedin.com/comm/jobs/view/4355741180/?trackingId=Ibkn5t%2Bt6xLjAOMNRDaa%2FA%3D%3D...)

2. **Director of Business Operations** at Insait (Tel Aviv District) - [View Job](https://www.linkedin.com/comm/jobs/view/4355731112/?trackingId=KVtPkBbJ5ahF6xUQeYC9PA%3D%3D...)

3. **מנהל טראפיק וניהול פרויקטים בסוכנות קריאייטיב** at JobsSeek (Raanana) - [View Job](https://www.linkedin.com/comm/jobs/view/4365688350/?trackingId=WEUEe3E77UBA8xeNGO4gWQ%3D%3D...)

4. **Chief Executive Officer** at the Israeli Association for Skin Cancer and Melanoma - [View Job](https://www.linkedin.com/comm/jobs/view/4355393642/?trackingId=sNNmjLWMkvjfCLU%2BUJ4jhA%3D%3D...)

5. **Junior Software Engineer** at InfinityLabs R&D (Haifa & Northern Israel) - [View Job](https://www.linkedin.com/comm/jobs/view/4346745306/?trackingId=eKv8%2FPW856tKKYehG7AFFA%3D%3D...)

Best of luck in your job search! If you need any assistance or have questions, feel free to reach out.

Best regards,
[Your Name]
analysis.reason: This job alert contains multiple job opportunities that match specific job preferences, making it relevant for outreach and application follow-up.

strategy.plan: Send personalized emails to job seekers with targeted job listings based on preferences.
strategy.roi: Increased engagement and higher likelihood of job placement for candidates.

original.subject: חשבנו עליך כשראינו את המשרה הזו: מנהל /ת עבודה שלד מוסמך ומנוסה: שכר מעולה, רכב צמוד, קרן השתלמות כל האפשרויות הארציות במקום אחד לפרטים נוספים >>
original.body:
איגור שלום,
ברגעים אלו מעסיק העלה משרה חמה שנמצאה מתאימה להגדרות החיפוש שלך.
עדכון על כל משרה חמה שעולה לאתר מאפשר לך להיות הראשון לשלוח קורות חיים ובכך להגדיל את סכוייך למצוא עבודה.
מנהל /ת עבודה שלד מוסמך ומנוסה: שכר מעולה, רכב צמוד, קרן השתלמות כל האפשרויות הארציות במקום אחד
חברה: insite-hr
מקום העבודה: מספר אזורים
סוג משרה: משרה מלאה, פרילנס, אקדמאים ללא ניסיון, לדוברי שפות... 
אנחנו, ב-Insite-HR, מתמחים בגיוס עובדים לתפקידי מפתח בביצוע, ומחזיקים היום בעשרות תקנים פתוחים בחברות הקבלניות המובילות בישראל....
לצפייה במשרה
תזמון דיוורים הגדרות חיפוש צור קשר 077-6-000-000
להסרה מרשימת תפוצה לחץ כאן
זוהי פניה בדיוור ישיר בהתאם למאגר רשום בבעלות אול יו ניד בע"מ ("החברה"), שמספרו 700013539 אשר מטרתו הינה, בין היתר, לצרכי דיוור ישיר. המידע במאגר התקבל ממך במסגרת הצטרפותך ו/או חברותך ו/או הירשמותך לקבל את שירותי החברה באתר האינטרנט (כהגדרתו מטה). באפשרותך לבקש הסרתך מהמאגר על-ידי פנייה בהודעה בכתב למשרדי החברה בכתובת בני ברמן 2, מגדל עיר ימים, נתניה 4249330 או באמצעות מתן הודעה באתר האינטרנט של החברה בכתובת האינטרנט www.alljobs.co.il . ניתן גם לפנות אלינו בכתובת הדואר האלקטרוני nituk@alljobs.co.il או בטלפון 077-6000000. החברה תפעל לטפל בבקשתך בהתאם למועדים הקבועים בחוק הגנת הפרטיות, תשמ"א-1981.
ליצירת קשר לחץ כאן.

original.subject: Immediate Action Required on my-home-435112

Google Cloud Platform



MY CONSOLE




Warning Policy Violation



Immediate action required: Resources associated with your Google Cloud  
Platform / API project my-home-435112 are being restricted



Dear Developer,


We detected and will disable a publicly exposed Service Account  
authentication credential associated with the following Google Cloud  
Platform account:

telegram-bot-firestore@my-home-435112.iam.gserviceaccount.com with key ID  
652622aad0c8e166e240366ccfafe410bc0b34a1

This key was found at the following URL:  
https://github.com/Gonya990/antibridge_fixed/blob/543bc3e0f7de0cf2a90e5203a4eaca917ab86006/Projects/AI_Core/gcp-service-account.json

For a subset of abuse issues, we send abuse notifications in log format.  
Please login to your console and review this abuse notification in Cloud  
Logging.

To prevent potential security and abuse risks, this Service Account  
authentication credential will be disabled in accordance with your  
Organizational Policy for “Service account key exposure response”. Your  
Service Account Credential may have been inadvertently published in public  
sources or websites (for instance, the credentials were mistakenly uploaded  
to a service like GitHub). Please note that as the project/account owner,  
you are responsible for securing your resources.

Immediate action is required to secure your account(s). We strongly  
recommend that you take the following steps:


Log in to the Google Cloud Console and review the activity on your account.
Rotate the credential for the disabled Service Account.
Take immediate steps to ensure that your Service Account credentials are  
not embedded in public source code systems, stored in download directories,  
or unintentionally shared in other ways.

Learn more about responding to abuse notifications and warnings

The security of your Google Cloud Platform account(s) is important to us.  
You can find more information on securely using IAM here and also recommend  
best practices for keeping service accounts keys safe. Please let us know  
if you have additional questions by contacting Google Cloud Support.




Request an appeal




Cordially,

Your Google Cloud Platform/API Trust & Safety Team




Google


© 2019 Google LLC, 1600 Amphitheatre Parkway, Mountain View, CA 94043

This email is sent to the owner of the project my-home-435112

You have received this mandatory service announcement to update you about  
important changes to Google Cloud Platform or your account.

analysis.subject: Urgent: Secrets Detected in GitHub Repository - Immediate Action Required
analysis.reply_body:
Dear GitHub Support,

Thank you for your alert regarding the detection of sensitive secrets in my GitHub repository 'Gonya990/antibridge_fixed'. I understand the urgency of the situation and will take immediate steps to audit the repository and rotate the compromised credentials to ensure security. Please advise if there are any additional measures you would recommend.

Best regards,
Gonya990
analysis.reason: The presence of sensitive information in the repository may lead to security breaches, requiring careful handling and user intervention.

strategy.plan: 1. Review the identified secrets in each location. 2. Rotate the compromised secrets immediately. 3. Update security practices to prevent future exposure. 4. Communicate with GitHub support for further guidance and confirmation of resolution.
strategy.roi: Securing sensitive information will prevent potential data breaches and protect user privacy, maintaining the integrity of the application.

analysis.subject: Application for Head of Operations at Ozio Limited
analysis.reply_body:
Dear Hiring Manager,

I am writing to express my interest in the Head of Operations position at Ozio Limited as advertised on LinkedIn. With my extensive experience in operations management and project management, particularly in the technology sector, I am confident in my ability to contribute effectively to your team.

I have a proven track record of optimizing operational processes, leading cross-functional teams, and driving innovative solutions that enhance productivity and efficiency. My skills in AI and automation align with your company's vision for growth and operational excellence.

Please find my resume attached for your consideration. I look forward to the opportunity to discuss my candidacy further.

Best regards,
Igor Goncharenko
[Your Phone Number]
[Your LinkedIn Profile Link]
analysis.reason: Automated response for job application based on the job alert.

strategy.plan: Send a tailored email expressing interest in the position and attach the resume.
strategy.roi: Potential job opportunity that aligns with the recipient's expertise.

analysis.subject: Job Application Follow-up for Opportunities in Israel
analysis.reply_body:
Dear Hiring Manager,

I am writing to express my interest in the recent job opportunities available in Israel that were highlighted in a job alert I received.

I am particularly interested in the positions for 'Head of Operations' with Ozio Limited in Raanana and 'Project Manager' with Oscar in the Center District. My background in operations management and project delivery aligns well with the responsibilities outlined for these roles.

I have attached my resume for your review and would appreciate the opportunity to discuss how I can contribute to your team.

Thank you for your consideration.

Best regards,
[Your Name]
[Your LinkedIn Profile]
[Your Phone Number]
analysis.reason: The email from LinkedIn Job Alerts contains a job alert for multiple positions that the user can apply to, and it's suitable for a standard follow-up application email.

strategy.plan: 1. Highlight interest in specific roles from the job alert. 2. Attach resume and offer to discuss further. 3. Send the email to prospective employers based on job listings.
strategy.roi: Engaging with multiple job opportunities may increase the chances of securing interviews and job placements.

I am writing to express my interest in the Director of Operations and Support position at Helfy, as recently alerted by LinkedIn. With extensive experience in operations management and team leadership, I am excited about the opportunity to contribute to your growing organization. 

Please find my resume attached for your review. I look forward to the opportunity to discuss my application further.

Best regards,
Igor Goncharenko
analysis.reason: This is a job alert for a relevant position matching the user's experience, making it suitable for a direct application.

strategy.plan: Reach out with a personalized email expressing interest in the role and attaching the resume.
strategy.roi: Maximize the opportunity to secure a leadership position by leveraging professional qualifications.

original.subject: חשבנו עליך כשראינו את המשרה הזו: דרוש /ה מלגזן לחברה מצליחה בתחומה בתחום ייצור ותעשייה לפרטים נוספים >>
original.body:
איגור שלום,
ברגעים אלו מעסיק העלה משרה חמה שנמצאה מתאימה להגדרות החיפוש שלך.
עדכון על כל משרה חמה שעולה לאתר מאפשר לך להיות הראשון לשלוח קורות חיים ובכך להגדיל את סכוייך למצוא עבודה.
דרוש /ה מלגזן לחברה מצליחה בתחומה בתחום ייצור ותעשייה
חברה: ר.ג.ת. לעבודות מתכת בע"מ
מקום העבודה: מספר אזורים
סוג משרה: משרה מלאה
חברת ר.ג.ת לעבודות מתכת מחפשת מלגזנים לצוות

אופי התפקיד:

קבלת סחורה או פירוק סחורה.
העמסת חומרים למשאית.
לשמור על...
לצפייה במשרה
תזמון דיוורים הגדרות חיפוש צור קשר 077-6-000-000
להסרה מרשימת תפוצה לחץ כאן
זוהי פניה בדיוור ישיר בהתאם למאגר רשום בבעלות אול יו ניד בע"מ ("החברה"), שמספרו 700013539 אשר מטרתו הינה, בין היתר, לצרכי דיוור ישיר. המידע במאגר התקבל ממך במסגרת הצטרפותך ו/או חברותך ו/או הירשמותך לקבל את שירותי החברה באתר האינטרנט (כהגדרתו מטה). באפשרותך לבקש הסרתך מהמאגר על-ידי פנייה בהודעה בכתב למשרדי החברה בכתובת בני ברמן 2, מגדל עיר ימים, נתניה 4249330 או באמצעות מתן הודעה באתר האינטרנט של החברה בכתובת האינטרנט www.alljobs.co.il . ניתן גם לפנות אלינו בכתובת הדואר האלקטרוני nituk@alljobs.co.il או בטלפון 077-6000000. החברה תפעל לטפל בבקשתך בהתאם למועדים הקבועים בחוק הגנת הפרטיות, תשמ"א-1981.
ליצירת קשר לחץ כאן.

analysis.subject: Job Opportunities for Operations & Office Manager Roles in Israel
analysis.reply_body:
Dear [Recipient's Name],

I hope this message finds you well. I am writing to express my interest in the Operations & Office Manager position at Datumate and other roles mentioned in the job alert, such as the Live Ops Manager position at PAPAYA and Shorebase Supervisor at Chevron. I believe my skills and experience align well with these roles, and I would love to discuss them further. 

Please let me know if there are any application processes I should follow or if you require further information from my side.

Thank you for your time and consideration. 

Best regards,
[Your Name]
[Your LinkedIn Profile] (if applicable)
[Your Contact Information]
analysis.reason: The job alert presents several relevant job openings in operations management. Responding to them aligns with business development opportunities.

strategy.plan: 1. Optimize the email for the targeted roles. 2. Send out applications to multiple roles at once to increase chances. 3. Tailor the email body for each specific position if necessary.
strategy.roi: By applying to multiple job openings, we can increase the chances of securing a suitable role quickly, thereby enhancing professional growth.

analysis.subject: Exciting Executive Opportunities in Israel
analysis.reply_body:
Dear Candidate,

I hope this message finds you well. I wanted to bring your attention to some exciting job opportunities in Israel that match your executive experience:

1. **Co-Founder/CEO - CortQ, Tel Aviv** - A top applicant position where you can lead innovative developments in the sector. For more details, [click here](https://www.linkedin.com/comm/jobs/view/4354180257/?trackingId=mkhD%2BTyGxUn4lQsfnbUY8w%3D%3D).

2. **Strategic Territory Director - Motorola Solutions, Israel** - This company is actively hiring, providing a chance to contribute to significant business strategies. For more details, [click here](https://www.linkedin.com/comm/jobs/view/4329609264/?trackingId=vWvz4W5USmJIf8m9Uv9Mqw%3D%3D).

I hope this message finds you well. I came across your recent job postings and am very interested in the positions available, particularly the Operations Maintenance Manager and Program Manager roles. I believe my skills and experience align closely with what you are seeking. 

I have attached my resume for your review. Please let me know if there are any further steps I should take.

Looking forward to your response.

Best regards,

strategy.plan: Send out applications for the selected roles using a standard email format. Customize the email slightly for each job application based on the position's requirements.
strategy.roi: Expanding job opportunities can significantly increase the chances of landing an interview and ultimately secure a role in a company with growth potential in Israel.

מנהל/ת תשתיות מפעלי
חברה: מש"א / הדרכה ...  |  אזור: צפון
>
משרה אחת  לצפייה במשרה
המשרות אליהן פנית
לא שלחת קורות חיים היום.
להשגת תוצאות טובות יותר, מומלץ לעבור על הגדרות החיפוש שלך ולעדכן במידת הצורך.
תזמון דיוורים הגדרות חיפוש צור קשר 077-6-000-000
להסרה מרשימת תפוצה לחץ כאן
זוהי פניה בדיוור ישיר בהתאם למאגר רשום בבעלות אול יו ניד בע"מ ("החברה"), שמספרו 700013539 אשר מטרתו הינה, בין היתר, לצרכי דיוור ישיר. המידע במאגר התקבל ממך במסגרת הצטרפותך ו/או חברותך ו/או הירשמותך לקבל את שירותי החברה באתר האינטרנט (כהגדרתו מטה). באפשרותך לבקש הסרתך מהמאגר על-ידי פנייה בהודעה בכתב למשרדי החברה בכתובת בני ברמן 2, מגדל עיר ימים, נתניה 4249330 או באמצעות מתן הודעה באתר האינטרנט של החברה בכתובת האינטרנט www.alljobs.co.il . ניתן גם לפנות אלינו בכתובת הדואר האלקטרוני nituk@alljobs.co.il או בטלפון 077-6000000. החברה תפעל לטפל בבקשתך בהתאם למועדים הקבועים בחוק הגנת הפרטיות, תשמ"א-1981.
ליצירת קשר לחץ כאן.

analysis.subject: Exciting Job Opportunities in Israel
analysis.reply_body:
Dear Hiring Manager,

I hope this message finds you well. I am reaching out to express my interest in the job opportunities currently available in Israel, particularly in roles such as Agile Manager and Project Manager. With my extensive experience in project management and agile methodologies, I believe I can contribute significantly to your team.

Please find my resume attached for your consideration. I look forward to the possibility of discussing these opportunities further.

Thank you for your time!

Best regards,
[Your Name]
[Your Phone Number]
[Your LinkedIn Profile]
[Your Email]
analysis.reason: The job alert indicates several opportunities that match interests, making it relevant for outreach.

strategy.plan: Identify desired positions and target companies, craft a tailored message for each position, and follow up with applications as needed.
strategy.roi: Increased chances of landing a suitable position through proactive outreach.

original.subject: Start building your enterprise structure 🧱
original.body:
Explore our recommendations     

‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌     

"GitHub"                                  "GitHub"                                            <https://app.github.media/e/er?s=88570519&lid=263&elqTrackId=3beeddfb1037496e83f72d4bc926e898&elq=f8741f2d11374e559513b43f1e33cc61&elqaid=4399&elqat=1&elqak=8AF5363E9793D1D4A1A3EE68E9980C3B0002D71FBCBE6F31733F8D0C5A825F43E96C>                            

Manage users, organizations, and repositories                   
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Setting up the account structure in GitHub Enterprise Cloud (GHEC) is a way of organizing and managing users, orgs, and repos. This setup can enhance the collaborative efficiency and security in your enterprise.                   

✅                                                
Start adding organizations                         
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Decide on an organization structure by exploring GitHub-recommended strategies and start adding organizations to your enterprise.                         

Explore account structure <https://app.github.media/e/er?s=88570519&lid=7248&elqTrackId=b6de069f6dbf4e84a57c7d11f5711438&elq=f8741f2d11374e559513b43f1e33cc61&elqaid=4399&elqat=1&elqak=8AF59C6C29405BA53731A589F62D568ED185D71FBCBE6F31733F8D0C5A825F43E96C>                                                            

👥                                                
Assign roles                         
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Adding more owners helps maintain ownership continuity for your organization. Learn which roles you can assign to control access to your enterprise's settings and data.                         

Learn about roles <https://app.github.media/e/er?s=88570519&lid=8324&elqTrackId=671a1f942cb644ecaa490818e6dc2fba&elq=f8741f2d11374e559513b43f1e33cc61&elqaid=4399&elqat=1&elqak=8AF583531895B7D591356C6CD65E758AC1B7D71FBCBE6F31733F8D0C5A825F43E96C>                                                            

🔐                                                
Govern your repositories                         
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Creating a repository policy enables you to control who can do things like create and delete repositories.                         

Govern repo usage <https://app.github.media/e/er?s=88570519&lid=8323&elqTrackId=45396b1d94e149b299af385fccfe9059&elq=f8741f2d11374e559513b43f1e33cc61&elqaid=4399&elqat=1&elqak=8AF5B3757E2AE015265572DF360C2510E9C6D71FBCBE6F31733F8D0C5A825F43E96C>                                                            

Want to skip ahead?                   
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Take a look at our onboarding checklist to see what the next steps are.                   

🟢  Get access and set up your enterprise <https://app.github.media/e/er?s=88570519&lid=7246&elqTrackId=0f9b52dc50db4db98583ef279051aaac&elq=f8741f2d11374e559513b43f1e33cc61&elqaid=4399&elqat=1&elqak=8AF5B2B4076839FA903B4810D64690644611D71FBCBE6F31733F8D0C5A825F43E96C>                         

🟢  Build account structure <https://app.github.media/e/er?s=88570519&lid=8320&elqTrackId=ebbc056b08c84b07b90b5a609e0685bc&elq=f8741f2d11374e559513b43f1e33cc61&elqaid=4399&elqat=1&elqak=8AF51489C4C9EFDF4D25255D11876CD552C0D71FBCBE6F31733F8D0C5A825F43E96C>                         

🟢  Migration checklist <https://app.github.media/e/er?s=88570519&lid=7244&elqTrackId=9fe8c3f2e690447c93452f0c38d43b56&elq=f8741f2d11374e559513b43f1e33cc61&elqaid=4399&elqat=1&elqak=8AF5269DD9ACACBBB8E3B217C9347C69ABFFD71FBCBE6F31733F8D0C5A825F43E96C>                         

🟢  Managing integrations <https://app.github.media/e/er?s=88570519&lid=7243&elqTrackId=95c87842022b4c39b0e55897565f706e&elq=f8741f2d11374e559513b43f1e33cc61&elqaid=4399&elqat=1&elqak=8AF5A19F1E9ABDFF32CA64AB86D44F5588A3D71FBCBE6F31733F8D0C5A825F43E96C>                                                   

🟢  Set business rules and regulations <https://app.github.media/e/er?s=88570519&lid=8319&elqTrackId=eab52690f87a4f6b881b0223e5d0ff95&elq=f8741f2d11374e559513b43f1e33cc61&elqaid=4399&elqat=1&elqak=8AF5F8BC4A7845C83E0F4C6807779C7F816BD71FBCBE6F31733F8D0C5A825F43E96C>                                                   

🟢  Get help <https://app.github.media/e/er?s=88570519&lid=7241&elqTrackId=0f9b52dc50db4db98583ef279051aaac&elq=f8741f2d11374e559513b43f1e33cc61&elqaid=4399&elqat=1&elqak=8AF5C9A23FEE6EE41F1C2AFDFC246B983C3CD71FBCBE6F31733F8D0C5A825F43E96C>                                                   

"X"                     <https://app.github.media/e/er?s=88570519&lid=7147&elqTrackId=933d4fa481eb46b1965378ace8196f6f&elq=f8741f2d11374e559513b43f1e33cc61&elqaid=4399&elqat=1&elqak=8AF551DD303AD64647FB2A99BACC91A41CA4D71FBCBE6F31733F8D0C5A825F43E96C>                                                                 "Facebook"                     <https://app.github.media/e/er?s=88570519&lid=3014&elqTrackId=e72f424494ed43548eba01ac5990fd54&elq=f8741f2d11374e559513b43f1e33cc61&elqaid=4399&elqat=1&elqak=8AF5F55B43C566CA58535E6FD0C44BC78FE0D71FBCBE6F31733F8D0C5A825F43E96C>                                                                 "YouTube"                     <https://app.github.media/e/er?s=88570519&lid=7146&elqTrackId=56c5de27d98642ab82743817066d926e&elq=f8741f2d11374e559513b43f1e33cc61&elqaid=4399&elqat=1&elqak=8AF5FE3B42D30C6824426EC6C3EAD33489C9D71FBCBE6F31733F8D0C5A825F43E96C>                                                                 "LinkedIn"                     <https://app.github.media/e/er?s=88570519&lid=3012&elqTrackId=d84860081dd441978f6a136c0e780e10&elq=f8741f2d11374e559513b43f1e33cc61&elqaid=4399&elqat=1&elqak=8AF562539DA98737A8CF652B3943DAAF686DD71FBCBE6F31733F8D0C5A825F43E96C>                                                                 "GitHub"                     <https://app.github.media/e/er?s=88570519&lid=47&elqTrackId=f3ced7077e6d44199137a700c5d4ae9b&elq=f8741f2d11374e559513b43f1e33cc61&elqaid=4399&elqat=1&elqak=8AF5BCE6C9182BA2CBDDE1836CF45C64DDFDD71FBCBE6F31733F8D0C5A825F43E96C>                   
Privacy policy <https://app.github.media/e/er?s=88570519&lid=7145&elqTrackId=1dfe816a405145fdb454b31c3bcaf0c9&elq=f8741f2d11374e559513b43f1e33cc61&elqaid=4399&elqat=1&elqak=8AF53F1987F153C7266A8EC71638B13623DDD71FBCBE6F31733F8D0C5A825F43E96C>                     •                     Terms of use <https://app.github.media/e/er?s=88570519&lid=7144&elqTrackId=74eaad70ec4f4605ae613f044f44c1d5&elq=f8741f2d11374e559513b43f1e33cc61&elqaid=4399&elqat=1&elqak=8AF534A483F7D23287DDA6D7CBDAD094F507D71FBCBE6F31733F8D0C5A825F43E96C>                     •                     Contact us <https://app.github.media/e/er?s=88570519&lid=7143&elqTrackId=9211067a6b3941dcbe6b17b455d48718&elq=f8741f2d11374e559513b43f1e33cc61&elqaid=4399&elqat=1&elqak=8AF52D379CF50A4FD60B6ED5F1C856EA3D0ED71FBCBE6F31733F8D0C5A825F43E96C>                     •                     Unsubscribe <https://app.github.media/e/u?s=88570519&elq=f8741f2d11374e559513b43f1e33cc61&elqak=8AF56BA7C6108615C570BD3710CB7C8D7E20D71FBCBE6F31733F8D0C5A825F43E96C>

analysis.subject: Get Started with Your Enterprise Structure on GitHub
analysis.reply_body: Explore our recommendations for building your enterprise structure on GitHub. This includes managing users, organizations, and repositories to enhance collaboration and security in your enterprise.
analysis.reason: This email contains promotional content and recommendations, not a direct lead for business development.

strategy.plan: Review the recommendations for potential use in marketing strategies, but no immediate action needed.
strategy.roi: Potential long-term benefits from increased awareness of GitHub services.

analysis.subject: New Job Opportunities for Director of Operations and Related Roles
analysis.reply_body:
Dear Hiring Manager,

I hope this message finds you well. I came across your recent job postings for the roles of Head of Customer Operations at Cust2Mate, Director of Engineering Operations at monday.com, Senior Director of Logistics at SolarEdge Technologies, and the Head of Project Management Division. I am very interested in these opportunities and would like to explore them further.

Please let me know how I can proceed with my application or if you would like to schedule a discussion.

Thank you for your time and consideration.

Best regards,
[Your Name]
[Your LinkedIn Profile]
[Your Contact Information]
analysis.reason: This email contains job alerts which are relevant for job applications. The action can be automated as there are no sensitive topics involved.

strategy.plan: Send an automated application email expressing interest in the roles listed.
strategy.roi: By applying to multiple roles in a single response, we streamline the process and increase the chances of securing a suitable position.

original.subject: Thanks for applying to Google
original.body:
Hi Igor Ivan Goncharenko SR,

Thanks for applying to Google! There are a ton of great companies out  
there, so we appreciate your interest in joining our team.

While we’re not able to reach out to every applicant, our recruiting team  
will contact you if your skills and experience are a strong match for the  
role. In the meantime, join the conversation about job opportunities and  
life at Google on our LinkedIn page:  
https://www.linkedin.com/company/google/.

We appreciate your interest in joining us.

Google Recruiting

analysis.subject: Application Follow-Up - Google
analysis.reply_body:
Hi Igor Ivan Goncharenko SR,

Thanks for applying to Google! There are a ton of great companies out there, so we appreciate your interest in joining our team.

While we’re not able to reach out to every applicant, our recruiting team will contact you if your skills and experience are a strong match for the role. In the meantime, join the conversation about job opportunities and life at Google on our LinkedIn page: https://www.linkedin.com/company/google/.

We appreciate your interest in joining us.

Google Recruiting
analysis.reason: This is a standard application acknowledgment email, which can be automatically sent out to candidates after they apply.

strategy.plan: Respond to applicants with a thank you message and direct them to the LinkedIn page for ongoing engagement.
strategy.roi: Enhances the candidate experience and promotes company brand awareness.

analysis.subject: Application for Engineering Operations Manager at Tehiru Aerial Systems
analysis.reply_body: Dear Hiring Manager, I am writing to express my interest in the Engineering Operations Manager position at Tehiru Aerial Systems as advertised on LinkedIn. With my extensive experience in engineering operations and a strong background in drone systems, I am confident in my ability to contribute effectively to your team. Please find my resume attached for your consideration. I look forward to the opportunity to discuss my application further. Best regards, [Your Name] [Your LinkedIn Profile] [Your Contact Information]
analysis.reason: The job alert from LinkedIn is directly relevant to a job application opportunity based on the recipient's preferences.

strategy.plan: Send an application email along with a resume highlighting relevant experience.
strategy.roi: Increased chances of landing an interview for a managerial position in a rapidly growing industry.

original.subject: איגור, עלו 2 משרות חדשות שיכולות להתאים לך
original.body:
איגור שלום,
להלן משרות שפורסמו באתר ומתאימות להגדרות החיפוש שלך
משרה חמה!
דרוש /ה נהג 12 טון לחברה מובילה בכרמיאל- 9,500 ש"ח נטו + רכב צמוד!!
חברה: morejobs
מקום העבודה: מספר אזורים
סוג משרה: משרה מלאה, מתאים גם לחיילים משוחררים, מתאים גם לבני 50 פלוס, מתאים גם למגזר הדתי... 
לחברה מובילה בתחום הספקה טכנית וכלי עבודה דרוש/ה נהג/ת 12 טון (על וואן) לעבודת חלוקה.
התקפיד כולל אספקת סחורה טכנית ללקוחות...
לצפייה במשרה
משרה חמה!
לחברה בתחום ציוד ההרמה והשינוע דרושה פקידת שירות
חברה: לימאן משאבי אנוש בע"מ
מקום העבודה: יקנעם
סוג משרה: משרה מלאה
לחברה בתחום ציוד ההרמה והשינוע דרושה פקידת שירות פרונטלית לעבודה משרדית מגוונת וסביבת עבודה מקצועית ונעימה.

תיאור התפקיד:

פתיחת...
לצפייה במשרה
לצפייה בכל המשרות בפיד שלך
תזמון דיוורים הגדרות חיפוש צור קשר 077-6-000-000
להסרה מרשימת תפוצה לחץ כאן
זוהי פניה בדיוור ישיר בהתאם למאגר רשום בבעלות אול יו ניד בע"מ ("החברה"), שמספרו 700013539 אשר מטרתו הינה, בין היתר, לצרכי דיוור ישיר. המידע במאגר התקבל ממך במסגרת הצטרפותך ו/או חברותך ו/או הירשמותך לקבל את שירותי החברה באתר האינטרנט (כהגדרתו מטה). באפשרותך לבקש הסרתך מהמאגר על-ידי פנייה בהודעה בכתב למשרדי החברה בכתובת בני ברמן 2, מגדל עיר ימים, נתניה 4249330 או באמצעות מתן הודעה באתר האינטרנט של החברה בכתובת האינטרנט www.alljobs.co.il . ניתן גם לפנות אלינו בכתובת הדואר האלקטרוני nituk@alljobs.co.il או בטלפון 077-6000000. החברה תפעל לטפל בבקשתך בהתאם למועדים הקבועים בחוק הגנת הפרטיות, תשמ"א-1981.
ליצירת קשר לחץ כאן.

original.subject: איזה כיף! קורות החיים שלך התקבלו בלוג-און תוכנה!
original.body:
היי, כאן חברת לוג-און תוכנה! קיבלנו את קורות החיים ששלחת ונשמח להתחיל בתהליך הגיוס 😊נתחיל בראיון קצר עם לוגי, המגייס הדיגיטלי שלנו! הריאיון יעזור לנו להכיר אותך יותר טוב ולהבין מהו הניסיון התעסוקתי שלך.לאחר סיום הריאיון, לוגי יבנה את הפרופיל המדויק שלך, אשר יועבר לצוות הגיוס שלנו ויוצלב בכל רגע נתון עם מגוון תפקידים שיכולים להתאים לך.את הצעות העבודה הרלוונטיות ביותר נשלח לך ישירות לווטסאפ בצורה סופר נוחה! אז שנתקדם?אם קיבלת מאיתנו הודעת SMS/WhatsApp – ניתן להתחיל את התהליך המתואר גם במענה להודעה הזו.במידה ולא קיבלת מאיתנו הודעה – אפשר להתחיל את השיחה עם לוגי ממש כאן >>> הקישור יוביל אותך לשליחת הודעת ווטסאפ ללוגי (״היי לוגי, אשמח לעזרתך״). בתפריט שמתקבל בוחרים בסעיף 1 ומשם לוגי יסביר לך בדיוק מה לעשות 😊חשוב להשלים את הריאיון עד לסופו על מנת שכל הפרטים המתאימים ייקלטו במערכות שלנו. המון בהצלחה,
אם אינך רוצה יותר לקבל התראות אלו, תוכל לנהל העדפות או לבטל את הרישום .
2016-2026 Olivia by <a href="https://www.paradox.ai/powered-by-paradox" style="text-decoration: none; color: #999999;"><b>Paradox</b></a>
6330 E Thomas Rd, Scottsdale, AZ 85251
